subtasks.title: Implement R2 control-plane wrappers in lib/r2/control.ts for list, delete, multipart, and bucket listing
subtasks.description: Create server-side wrappers around the needed S3 commands, including pagination support and batched deletes.
subtasks.details:
Add `import 'server-only'`. Export concrete async functions with typed params/returns: `listObjects(params: { client: S3Client; bucket: string; prefix?: string; continuationToken?: string; maxKeys?: number }): Promise<{ items: Array<{ key: string; size?: number; etag?: string; lastModified?: Date }>; continuationToken?: string; isTruncated: boolean }>`, `deleteObjects(params: { client: S3Client; bucket: string; keys: string[] }): Promise<{ deleted: string[]; errors: Array<{ key?: string; code?: string; message?: string }> }>`, `createMultipartUpload(params: { client: S3Client; bucket: string; key: string; contentType?: string }): Promise<{ uploadId: string }>`, `completeMultipartUpload(params: { client: S3Client; bucket: string; key: string; uploadId: string; parts: Array<{ partNumber: number; etag: string }> }): Promise<{ etag?: string; location?: string }>`, `abortMultipartUpload(params: { client: S3Client; bucket: string; key: string; uploadId: string }): Promise<void>`, and `listBuckets(params: { client: S3Client }): Promise<Array<{ name?: string; creationDate?: Date }>>`. Only import the command classes used: `ListObjectsV2Command`, `DeleteObjectsCommand`, `CreateMultipartUploadCommand`, `CompleteMultipartUploadCommand`, `AbortMultipartUploadCommand`, and `ListBucketsCommand`. For `listObjects`, forward `ContinuationToken` and return next token from `NextContinuationToken`. For `deleteObjects`, validate keys, short-circuit empty arrays, and chunk requests into batches of at most 1000 keys per command, aggregating deleted entries and per-key errors across responses. For `completeMultipartUpload`, validate non-empty parts, normalize/sort by `partNumber`, and map to AWS `CompletedPart` shape with `ETag`. Catch SDK errors in each wrapper and convert them via `mapR2Error`. Keep server-only and minimal-import discipline for edge runtime compatibility.
<info added on 2026-05-21T04:46:24.157Z>
Implemented lib/r2/control.ts and tests/unit/r2/control.test.ts. Added server-only control-plane wrappers matching the specified surface exactly: listObjects, deleteObjects, createMultipartUpload, completeMultipartUpload, abortMultipartUpload, and listBuckets, with tight imports limited to the six S3 command classes used and S3Client imported type-only for edge-bundle discipline.

Implementation notes:
Validation helpers are inlined locally, mirroring presign.ts, and throw TypeError for programmer/input bugs before any SDK call; only actual client.send failures are mapped through mapR2Error.
listObjects forwards ContinuationToken, returns NextContinuationToken as continuationToken, and defensively filters out entries with empty/missing Key so every returned item has a non-empty key.
deleteObjects validates inputs, short-circuits empty key arrays with { deleted: [], errors: [] }, splits requests into batches of at most 1000 keys, and aggregates deleted entries plus per-key errors across batch responses. Processing uses per-batch try/catch so successful earlier batches are not discarded before a later failure is rethrown.
completeMultipartUpload validates non-empty parts plus every partNumber/etag, copies and sorts parts by ascending partNumber before sending, maps to AWS CompletedPart with ETag, and preserves caller array immutability.
createMultipartUpload conditionally includes ContentType and explicitly throws if UploadId is absent from an otherwise successful SDK response.
listBuckets returns [] when the SDK response omits Buckets.

Test coverage added in tests/unit/r2/control.test.ts:
Uses a lightweight fake client with send: vi.fn() and real AWS command classes to assert command input shapes without adding new test dependencies.
Covers list pagination mapping, deleteObjects validation, empty-array short-circuit, 1001-key batching boundary, multi-batch aggregation of deleted/errors, multipart create/complete/abort behavior, part sorting without mutating caller input, listBuckets empty/missing-field handling, and error mapping across multiple wrappers for both credential and generic upstream failures.

pnpm lint clean

subtasks.title: Create connection Zod schemas, route param validation, and access key masking utilities
subtasks.description: Define the shared validation schemas and masking helpers required by all connection CRUD routes.
subtasks.details:
Add Zod schemas for POST payload, PATCH payload, and connection id params in a shared server module. Include the exact accountId regex, name length constraints, and credential minimum lengths. Implement a pure masking utility that returns accessKeyId.slice(0,4) + '****' + accessKeyId.slice(-4), and export typed helpers so route handlers can reuse validation and safe response shaping without duplicating logic.
<info added on 2026-05-21T06:09:57.422Z>
In lib/api/schemas.ts, add ConnectionsCreateSchema as a strict object with { name, accountId, accessKeyId, secretAccessKey }, using accountId regex /^[a-f0-9]{32}$/ to match the Cloudflare R2 account ID format from the brief. Add ConnectionsPatchSchema as a strict object that only allows { name }, so any attempt to PATCH accountId or access keys is rejected by Zod with 400 before handler logic. Add ConnectionIdParamSchema validating id as a ULID. Export a pure maskAccessKey helper that returns accessKeyId.slice(0,4) + '****' + accessKeyId.slice(-4); handlers can use it when writing masked values, while GET routes continue reading access_key_masked directly from the DB.

In lib/api/errors.ts, add ApiErrorCode entries and factory helpers for connection.invalid_credentials and connection.in_use. connectionInvalidCredentials should return 400, because the user session is valid and only the supplied R2 credentials were rejected. connectionInUse should return 409 to represent a resource-conflict condition blocking deletion when the connection is still referenced.

In lib/audit/log.ts, extend AuditOp to include 'connection.update' so PATCH rename operations can be logged consistently with the rest of the connection CRUD audit flow.
</info added on 2026-05-21T06:09:57.422Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test schema success/failure cases and masking output for normal and edge-length access keys.

subtasks.title: Implement POST /api/connections with Edge runtime, CSRF, rate limiting, R2 credential verification, encryption, and audit logging
subtasks.description: Build the create-connection API flow from request validation through R2 verification, encrypted persistence, and audit writes.
subtasks.details:
In app/api/connections/route.ts, export runtime = 'edge' and implement POST. Apply CSRF validation and rate-limit checks before processing writes. Parse the request with the shared POST schema, create or generate the connection id, call the R2 validation step using listBuckets once to confirm credentials, and on failure return the localized error key 'connection.invalid_credentials' without echoing any part of accessKeyId or secretAccessKey. On success, encrypt accessKeyId and secretAccessKey using encryptCredential(value, id), compute accessKeyMasked, insert the row into connections, and write audit_log entries for attempt, success, and failure as appropriate. Return only safe fields in the response.
<info added on 2026-05-21T06:33:43.816Z>
Wrap the POST handler with withApi and use RateLimitBundles.writeOnlyByUser as the single aggregated 600/min write budget for connection creation. Parse input via parseJson(ConnectionsCreateSchema, strict), and derive endpoint internally from accountId as https://{accountId}.r2.cloudflarestorage.com rather than accepting any client-supplied endpoint to avoid SSRF.

Generate the connection ULID before any encryption and use it as AAD for encryptCredential so both ciphertext values are bound to the specific connection row. After parsing, create the S3 client with the derived endpoint and probe credentials with exactly one listBuckets call.

On probe failure, distinguish R2CredentialError from other failures: map R2CredentialError to 400 with error key connection.invalid_credentials rather than 401, because auth/session is valid and only the provided R2 credentials were rejected. For probe-time audit logging, use connection_id = null because the row has not been inserted yet and a non-null FK would fail. Failure responses must not include any access key material or fragments; tests should assert JSON.stringify(responseBody) does not contain the full key, first 4 chars, or last 4 chars of either secret.

On success, encrypt accessKeyId and secretAccessKey with AAD = connection.id, convert ciphertext for Drizzle blob({ mode: 'buffer' }) columns via Buffer.from(...) under nodejs_compat, insert the connection, write audit success, and return 201 with a Location header pointing to the created resource.
</info added on 2026-05-21T06:33:43.816Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Vitest route tests for valid creation, invalid credentials returning connection.invalid_credentials, no secret fragments in response, and enforcement of CSRF/rate limiting on writes.

subtasks.title: Implement GET /api/connections list endpoint with masked safe response fields only
subtasks.description: Build the list API that returns connection summaries without exposing encrypted credential fields.
subtasks.details:
In app/api/connections/route.ts, add GET alongside POST with runtime = 'edge'. Query the current user's connections, select only id, name, accountId, accessKeyMasked, createdAt, and lastUsedAt, and return them as an array. Do not decrypt anything and do not include ciphertext, IV columns, secret fields, or any internal encryption metadata in the serialized response. Ensure response formatting is stable for the UI hook layer.
<info added on 2026-05-21T06:33:56.517Z>
Implement GET in app/api/connections/route.ts with export const runtime = 'edge' and wrap the handler with withApi. Do not apply rate limiting for this read endpoint. Query only rows where user_id equals ctx.userId and use an explicit select() list for exactly these six safe columns: id, name, accountId, accessKeyMasked, createdAt, lastUsedAt. Do not use select(*) so newly added secret or internal columns cannot be exposed by default. Add a rowToSummary mapper that converts createdAt and lastUsedAt Date values to epoch milliseconds to keep JSON stable across runtimes. Do not write audit_log for this endpoint because it is a high-frequency read path and the returned fields are not security-sensitive. Add tests that assert Object.keys() of each response item contains only the six allowed fields so any accidental extra field exposure is caught immediately.
</info added on 2026-05-21T06:33:56.517Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Vitest route test verifying returned objects include masked fields and omit ciphertext, iv, and secret credential columns.

subtasks.title: Implement PATCH /api/connections/[id] to allow name-only updates with Edge runtime, CSRF, rate limiting, and audit logging
subtasks.description: Add the update endpoint that only permits renaming an existing connection owned by the current user.
subtasks.details:
In app/api/connections/[id]/route.ts, export runtime = 'edge' and implement PATCH. Validate the route id and body with shared schemas, reject any unexpected fields so only name can change, and scope the update to the authenticated user's record. Apply CSRF and rate limiting before the write, update the name field only, handle missing records cleanly, and write audit_log entries for rename attempts, successes, and failures. Return the updated safe summary payload rather than any encrypted columns.
<info added on 2026-05-21T06:34:24.441Z>
Use ConnectionsPatchSchema.strict() so PATCH only accepts the name field; payloads like { name, accountId } must fail Zod validation with 400 without additional handler-side whitelisting. Because withApi narrows the handler to (req) => Response and drops Next.js 15 params context, parse the resource id from the last path segment via pathIdFrom(req.url) and then validate it as a ULID with Zod. Perform the update as a single UPDATE ... WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ? RETURNING statement scoped to the authenticated user; if no row is returned, respond with 404 and do not distinguish between nonexistent and other-user records, matching the non-enumeration behavior used by the presign route. Use audit op connection.update. Add a test assertion that after a cross-user PATCH attempt, the target row's name remains unchanged as a defense-in-depth check.
</info added on 2026-05-21T06:34:24.441Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Vitest route tests for successful rename, rejection of extra mutable fields like accountId/accessKeyId, not-found behavior, and CSRF/rate-limit enforcement.

subtasks.title: Implement DELETE /api/connections/[id] with share dependency checks, deletion policy, Edge runtime protections, and audit logging
subtasks.description: Add the delete endpoint with safeguards that prevent removing a connection still referenced by active shares.
subtasks.details:
In app/api/connections/[id]/route.ts, implement DELETE with runtime = 'edge'. Validate the id, enforce CSRF and rate limiting, then query shares using SELECT COUNT(*) for the current connection_id and the product rule for unexpired shares. By default, reject deletion when dependent shares exist and return a clear message instructing the user to remove shares first, unless product later chooses cascade behavior. If deletion is allowed, apply the chosen hard-delete or soft-delete strategy consistently with the schema and write audit_log entries for blocked attempts, successful deletion, and errors. Ensure ownership checks prevent deleting another user's connection.
<info added on 2026-05-21T06:34:54.096Z>
DELETE flow should be: validate ownership with an initial SELECT, then query active share count via SELECT COUNT(*) FROM shares WHERE connection_id = ? AND expires_at > now, reject with 409 connection.in_use when count > 0, otherwise write the success audit entry before executing DELETE. Do not allow cascade deletion at the API layer; product rule is that users must remove shares first.

Important FK constraint fix: audit_log.connection_id references connections.id, and ON DELETE SET NULL only applies to existing audit rows when the parent connection is deleted. It does not bypass FK validation for a new audit_log INSERT after the connection row is already gone. The previous delete-then-audit order caused SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_FOREIGNKEY, so success audit logging must happen before DELETE. This is acceptable because the prior SELECT confirms the row exists, DELETE is unlikely to fail, and logAudit is already best-effort/no-fail with console.error only.

Use drizzle gt(shares.expiresAt, now) so expired shares do not block deletion, since expired URLs are effectively dead links. Rely on ON DELETE CASCADE to clean up expired share rows automatically after the connection DELETE; no manual second delete is needed, and tests should assert related shares are removed. When blocked, ApiErrors.connectionInUse should include details: { activeShares: number } so the UI can display how many active shares are preventing deletion.
</info added on 2026-05-21T06:34:54.096Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Vitest route tests for delete blocked by active shares, successful delete when no related shares exist, unauthorized ownership protection, and audit side effects.

subtasks.title: Add comprehensive Vitest coverage for connection CRUD routes, security guards, and audit behavior
subtasks.description: Create a route-level test suite covering happy paths, validation errors, secret handling, and deletion constraints.
subtasks.details:
Add Vitest tests for app/api/connections/route.ts and app/api/connections/[id]/route.ts using mocked auth, DB, R2 validation, encryption, CSRF, rate limiting, and audit services. Cover POST invalid credentials, POST success with encrypted persistence and masked output, GET safe field projection, PATCH name-only updates, DELETE share checks, and ensure all write routes invoke CSRF/rate-limit/audit logic. Include regression assertions that responses never expose raw access keys, secret keys, ciphertext, or IV values.
<info added on 2026-05-21T06:35:10.214Z>
Add tests/unit/api/connections-route.test.ts with 13 tests following the existing r2-presign-route.test.ts “fat test” pattern: use real better-sqlite3, real drizzle schema, and real AES-GCM encryption, while mocking only the boundaries for next-auth/jwt, next-on-pages env, @/lib/r2/control listBuckets, and @/lib/r2/client makeS3Client. Reuse makeD1Facade to adapt the DB for RateLimitDb.

Cover this matrix:
POST: strict schema rejects extra fields; invalid accountId format; R2CredentialError returns 400 connection.invalid_credentials with assertions that both response and audit payload never contain any key fragments; success path persists ciphertext blobs instead of plaintext in DB, returns masked fields only, and records audit success; CSRF mismatch returns 401.
GET: enforce cross-user isolation; assert response contains only the 6 safe fields via Object.keys; unauthenticated request returns 401.
PATCH: successful rename updates only name and records audit; extra fields are rejected by strict schema and the row remains unchanged; cross-user PATCH returns 404 and leaves the row unchanged.
DELETE: active non-expired share blocks deletion with 409 and connection remains; when only expired shares exist deletion succeeds and cascades share cleanup; cross-user DELETE returns 404.

Validate the suite together with pnpm typecheck, lint, test, and build all passing; final run green with 241 tests passing.
</info added on 2026-05-21T06:35:10.214Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Run Vitest route suites with mocks/spies asserting status codes, JSON payloads, DB calls, audit invocations, and absence of sensitive fields in responses.

subtasks.title: Set up app-wide client providers for React Query and Sonner
subtasks.description: Create a shared client-side providers component and integrate it into the root app layout.
subtasks.details:
Add components/providers.tsx as a client component that initializes a stable QueryClient instance and wraps children with QueryClientProvider and Sonner Toaster. Update app/layout.tsx to mount this Providers wrapper so all dashboard and settings pages can use TanStack Query and toast notifications.
<info added on 2026-05-21T08:00:30.662Z>
In components/providers.tsx, initialize QueryClient with useState(() => new QueryClient(...)) to avoid SSR cross-request sharing. Configure defaultOptions with queries.staleTime = 30_000, queries.retry = 1, and mutations.retry = 0 so failed mutations are not retried and do not duplicate audit_log entries.

Wrap the provider tree with ThemeProvider using attribute="class", defaultTheme="light", and enableSystem={false}. This is required because components/ui/sonner.tsx uses useTheme(); without a ThemeProvider, useTheme() is undefined and production builds emit warnings even though Sonner falls back to "system" at runtime.

Provider nesting/order should be ThemeProvider -> QueryClientProvider -> Toaster + children, with Toaster rendered inside the QueryClientProvider subtree but above the children slot in Providers. If Toaster is rendered under children instead, toast() calls may not find the container.

Mount a single Providers wrapper directly under app/layout.tsx body and use it for all app children, including login and dashboard routes.
</info added on 2026-05-21T08:00:30.662Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Run the app and verify pages render without provider errors; confirm a child component using useQuery and a toast can mount successfully.

Include actions such as setActiveConnectionId and optional clearActiveConnectionId, ensuring no credential data is stored.
<info added on 2026-05-21T08:00:38.418Z>
Use zustand persist with createJSONStorage(() => localStorage) so storage access is lazy. Explicitly set partialize to persist only activeConnectionId and not actions. Add version: 1 now to reserve a future migration hook. subtasks.details:

Use lib/api/client.ts apiFetch for GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE calls, type request and response payloads from ConnectionSummary-compatible structures, surface ApiClientError correctly, and invalidate or optimistically update the connections query after mutations.

Refactor hooks/use-connections.ts into two layers: exported plain async fetchers fetchConnections, createConnection, updateConnection, and deleteConnection that call apiFetch directly, plus React hooks wrapping them with useQuery/useMutation. Export a shared singleton CONNECTIONS_QUERY_KEY = ['connections'] as const and use it consistently for useQuery, invalidateQueries, and setQueryData.

On mutation success, perform double cache updates: first setQueryData for immediate UI response, then invalidateQueries to refetch and reconcile any server-side normalization such as trimming or lowercasing. Cache update rules: create prepends the new row, update replaces by id via map, delete removes by id via filter.

Move ConnectionSummary out of app/api/connections/route.ts into a new browser-safe lib/api/types.ts, then update both client hooks and route files to import the type from there. This avoids Next.js client bundling errors caused by importing types from a server-only route module.

For the query key, verify with toEqual(['connections']) and a length check rather than Object.isFrozen, since as const does not freeze at runtime.

subtasks.title: Implement add, rename, and delete connection dialogs
subtasks.description: Create the three dialog/form components for connection creation, renaming, and typed deletion confirmation.
subtasks.details:
Add components/features/connections/add-connection-dialog.tsx, rename-connection-dialog.tsx, and delete-connection-dialog.tsx using shadcn Dialog and native useState forms. The add dialog should collect name, accountId, accessKeyId, and secretAccessKey, show loading during submit, and display sonner error toasts from ApiClientError messages such as connection.invalid_credentials. The rename dialog should only edit the name. The delete dialog must require exact connection name input before enabling the Delete button.
<info added on 2026-05-21T08:01:05.419Z>
Each dialog file should export one outer *Dialog component and one inner *Form component. The outer component only owns the Radix/shadcn Dialog shell and passes onClose as () => onOpenChange(false); all form state and mutation hooks must live in the inner Form component so DialogContent unmount on close resets state naturally. Do not use useEffect to reset form/mutation on open changes; this avoids the react-hooks/set-state-in-effect ESLint violation and follows the React 19 unmount-resets-state pattern.

Add dialog should perform client-side validation mirroring the server ConnectionsCreateSchema before submit: name required/max as applicable, accountId must match 32 lowercase hex chars, accessKeyId min length 20, secretAccessKey min length 40, to prevent guaranteed 400s. Error toasts should go through a shared describeError() helper that maps ApiClientError.code to user-facing messages, explicitly covering connection.invalid_credentials, connection.in_use, and not_found.

Delete confirmation must be intentionally strict: enable destructive submit only when confirmation === connection.name, with no trim and no case folding. This is deliberate for destructive safety and should not accept loosened pasted variants.

Cancel buttons in all dialogs should call the provided onClose callback instead of invoking onOpenChange directly with a boolean, to keep handler types consistent.
</info added on 2026-05-21T08:01:05.419Z>

subtasks.title: Implement read-only GET /api/r2/buckets Edge route with auth, decryption, and scoped bucket listing
subtasks.description: Create the buckets API route that validates cid, enforces user scope, decrypts stored R2 credentials, lists buckets, updates usage metadata, and returns safe bucket summaries.
subtasks.details:
Add app/api/r2/buckets/route.ts with runtime='edge'. Use existing session/auth middleware to require the current user, parse query input { cid }, load the connection only if it belongs to that user, decrypt access credentials server-side, create the S3/R2 client, call listBuckets, map results to [{ name, createdAt }], update connection.last_used_at, apply rate limiting, and emit audit events for success/failure. Return structured errors for connection.not_found and connection.invalid_credentials, and ensure no ciphertext, IV, secret, or raw provider error leaks into responses or logs.
<info added on 2026-05-21T11:34:43.061Z>
Implement app/api/r2/buckets/route.ts as GET with runtime = 'edge'. Validate query via parseQuery(req, R2BucketsQuerySchema) and add parseQuery to lib/api/schemas.ts as the query-string counterpart to parseJson, using Object.fromEntries(req.nextUrl.searchParams.entries()) so single-value params like cid are parsed correctly. Scope the connection lookup by both id and userId, matching the presign route behavior; if not found, return 404 connection.not_found to avoid ULID enumeration.

When reading encrypted credential blobs, reuse the same asU8 helper pattern from the presign route so both local Buffer and production ArrayBuffer values work under D1/better-sqlite3 differences. Decrypt access key and secret key in parallel with Promise.all, and pass connection.id as AES-GCM AAD to decryptCredential. If decryption fails, emit audit event security.decrypt_failed and return 500 internal without exposing CryptoIntegrityError details.

Create the R2/S3 client from decrypted credentials and call listBuckets. Map the response into BucketSummary objects in lib/api/types.ts with shape { name: string; createdAt: number | null }. Because ListBucketsCommand may return optional Name values, filter out undefined and empty-string names so only non-empty string bucket names are returned. Convert creation dates to epoch milliseconds or null.

Handle provider errors so R2CredentialError returns 401 auth.unauthorized; let other R2 errors bubble to withApi for generic 500 handling rather than leaking raw provider details. Do not add rate limiting here: TanStack Query's 5-minute stale window already suppresses client frequency, and if caching is removed later this route can reuse RateLimitPolicies.presignByUser. On success, update connection.last_used_at only after listBuckets succeeds, and wrap that update in try/catch so metadata write failure is non-fatal. Do not emit success audit logs for this read-only high-frequency endpoint, consistent with GET /api/connections.
</info added on 2026-05-21T11:34:43.061Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Vitest route tests with mocked DB, decryptCredential, makeS3Client, and listBuckets; assert scoped access, last_used_at update, audit calls, rate limit handling, and sanitized error responses.

<info added on 2026-05-21T12:46:04.512Z>
Update hooks/use-objects.ts to TanStack Query v5 with queryKey ['objects', cid, bucket, prefix], and extract the request logic into a standalone fetchObjects function so vitest can verify request URL shape independently.

Implementation decisions:
- Use a 4-tuple queryKey instead of 3-tuple so cid participates in caching; switching connections must create isolated caches and prevent same-named buckets across different connections from polluting each other.
- Define OBJECTS_STALE_TIME_MS = 60_000. This is shorter than the buckets cache (5 minutes) because object listings change more frequently, while still avoiding refetch on every window focus.
- When inputs are not ready (missing cid or bucket), set enabled: false so the hook stays idle and the UI can render its own “select connection” / missing-input prompt.

TanStack Query v5 / TypeScript pitfalls:
- v5 requires initialPageParam; pass undefined as string | undefined explicitly, otherwise pageParam is inferred as unknown.
- getNextPageParam must coerce terminal null to undefined. R2 uses null for the last page, but TanStack treats null as a valid next page param. Use lastPage.nextCursor ?? undefined.
- Keep the return type explicit as UseInfiniteQueryResult<InfiniteData<R2ListResponse, string | undefined>, ApiClientError | Error>; the pageParam generic must be provided or TS will error.

Also normalize queryKey inputs in tests so null and undefined are both treated as null for stable cache keys, while still encoding URL params correctly in fetchObjects.

Test coverage should include:
- queryKey normalization (null/undefined -> null)
- fetchObjects URL encoding for prefix/cursor values containing spaces and '+'
- first-page request vs continuation-page request parameter differences
- ApiClientError passthrough without wrapping/masking
</info added on 2026-05-21T12:46:04.512Z>

tasks.title: /api/r2/presign 端点
tasks.description: POST /api/r2/presign，输入 { cid, op: 'put'|'get'|'upload-part', bucket, key, ttl?, uploadId?, partNumber? }；服务端解密 -> 生成 presigned URL；ttl 默认 put=15min, get=15min；写一条 audit_log (presign.put/get)。
tasks.details: 1) app/api/r2/presign/route.ts；2) Zod discriminated union by op；3) put -> presignPut(ttl=900)，get -> presignGet(ttl=900)，upload-part -> presignUploadPart(ttl=900)；4) 限流：60/min/user（rate-limit.ts）；5) 返回 { url, expiresAt: Date.now() + ttl*1000 }；6) audit_log.op = `presign.${op==='get'?'get':'put'}`（part 也算 put 类）；7) ttl 上限 7200 (2 小时) 防滥用；8) URL 不入库（避免泄漏）。
tasks.testStrategy: Vitest：3 种 op 各返回合法 presigned URL；ttl 超 7200 返 400；触发限流第 61 次返 429 + Retry-After；audit_log 写入一行。

subtasks.title: Create request schema and route scaffold for POST /api/r2/presign
subtasks.description: Add the new API route file and define validated request parsing for all supported presign operations.
subtasks.details:
Create app/api/r2/presign/route.ts with a POST handler. Define a Zod discriminated union keyed by op to support put, get, and upload-part. Shared fields should include cid, bucket, key, and optional ttl, while upload-part must additionally require uploadId and partNumber. Prepare consistent JSON error responses for invalid input.
<info added on 2026-05-21T05:25:24.561Z>
In lib/api/schemas.ts, add shared constants R2_PRESIGN_MAX_TTL_SECONDS = 7200 and R2_PRESIGN_DEFAULT_TTL_SECONDS = 900 so route code, clients, and docs all reference one source of truth instead of duplicating magic numbers.

Define R2PresignBaseShape using the existing UlidSchema, BucketNameSchema, and ObjectKeySchema to keep cid validation aligned with the rest of the API and guarantee a valid Crockford base32 ULID. Use ttl: z.number().int().positive().max(R2_PRESIGN_MAX_TTL_SECONDS).optional() so 0, negatives, and decimals are rejected, 7200 is accepted, and 7201 is rejected.

Export R2PresignSchema as z.discriminatedUnion("op", [...]) rather than z.union, with put/get/upload-part variants sharing R2PresignBaseShape. The upload-part variant must additionally require uploadId: z.string().min(1).max(256) and partNumber: z.number().int().min(1).max(10_000). The 10_000 ceiling matches the S3 multipart part-number limit; the 256-char uploadId cap is a defensive guardrail for SDK URL builder stability rather than an S3 spec requirement.

In app/api/r2/presign/route.ts, set export const runtime = "edge" and export const POST = withApi(handler, { rateLimit: ... }). Inside handler, call await parseJson(req, R2PresignSchema) and rely on withApi to map any ZodError to the standard 400 response format.

Do not keep the schema local to route.ts: lib/api/schemas.ts is the intended centralized location, and the inferred type will be reused by future hooks/components. discriminatedUnion is also preferred for stronger TypeScript narrowing and better parser behavior for literal-tagged object unions.

Testing pitfall notes: use ulid() to generate cid values in tests, since a hand-written 27-character “ULID” will always fail validation. Also ensure bucket names satisfy BucketNameSchema minimum length; use a valid value like "buk" instead of "b" or tests will incorrectly fail at schema validation with 400 before reaching expected 404/429/500 paths.
</info added on 2026-05-21T05:25:24.561Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Vitest schema tests for valid/invalid payloads per op, including missing uploadId/partNumber for upload-part.

subtasks.title: Integrate authentication, connection lookup, and credential decryption
subtasks.description: Resolve the caller identity, load the target connection, and decrypt stored credentials before presigning.
subtasks.details:
Within the route handler, obtain the current user from the existing auth flow, enforce user-scoped access to the requested cid, and fetch the related connection record. Use the existing decryption utility from prior tasks to recover access credentials. Return appropriate errors for missing connections or decrypt failures, and avoid ever persisting or logging decrypted secrets or generated URLs.
<info added on 2026-05-21T05:25:33.918Z>
Use withApi so ctx.userId comes from the built-in D1 session validation and revocation-check middleware rather than re-resolving auth inside the handler.

Load the connection via drizzle with a strictly user-scoped predicate:
db.select().from(schema.connections).where(and(eq(schema.connections.id, cid), eq(schema.connections.userId, ctx.userId))).get()
The WHERE must include both id and user_id to prevent presigning with another user's guessed ULID.

If no matching connection is found, return ApiErrors.notFound() instead of 403. Cross-user access attempts must also resolve to 404, and tests expect that path to avoid writing any audit row under the other user's identity.

Decrypt access_key and secret_key in parallel with Promise.all using decryptCredential twice. Read env.ENCRYPTION_KEY from getRequestContext().env, use connection.id as AAD for both decryptions, and ensure argument order matches aes-gcm.ts: ciphertext first, iv second.

Normalize drizzle blob values with an asU8 helper before decryption. Cloudflare D1 may return ArrayBuffer while Node/Vitest returns Buffer (Uint8Array subclass); convert both to Uint8Array so decryptCredential receives the expected type.

Catch all decryption failures uniformly, write one audit row with op="security.decrypt_failed" and status="failure", then throw ApiErrors.internal(). Do not expose CryptoIntegrityError details or any AES-GCM integrity failure message to clients, per aes-gcm.ts guidance. Treat this as a server-side at-rest encryption integrity problem, not a user credential/auth problem, so do not map it to 401.

Do not switch this lookup path to raw env.DB queries; keep route-layer database access on drizzle for schema-coupled type safety and compile-time protection during schema changes.
</info added on 2026-05-21T05:25:33.918Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Vitest with mocked auth/DB/decrypt utilities covering authorized success, connection not found, and decrypt failure paths.

subtasks.title: Enforce rate limiting and TTL normalization rules
subtasks.description: Apply abuse-prevention checks before generating presigned URLs and compute safe expiration values.
subtasks.details:
Use rate-limit.ts to enforce 60 requests per minute per user for this endpoint. Normalize ttl by applying the default of 900 seconds for put/get/upload-part when absent, and clamp/reject values above 7200 seconds with a 400 response. Compute expiresAt as Date.now() + ttl * 1000 for successful responses. Include Retry-After when the limiter blocks the request.
<info added on 2026-05-21T05:25:40.609Z>
Move TTL constants into lib/api/schemas.ts as shared definitions: R2_PRESIGN_MAX_TTL_SECONDS = 7200 and R2_PRESIGN_DEFAULT_TTL_SECONDS = 900. Keep ttl in the presign input schema as z.number().int().positive().max(R2_PRESIGN_MAX_TTL_SECONDS).optional(); rely on withApi’s ZodError -> 400 mapping for over-limit/invalid ttl inputs, so the route must not duplicate manual 400 validation. In the route handler, only apply the fallback with input.ttl ?? R2_PRESIGN_DEFAULT_TTL_SECONDS, preserving omitted-vs-provided semantics instead of using .default(900) in schema.

Use withApi rate-limit options instead of route-local limiter code: rateLimit: ({ ctx }) => RateLimitBundles.presignByUser(ctx.userId). RateLimitBundles already orders the narrow presign 60/min policy before the broader write-aggregate 600/min policy and writes the first tripped policy into error.details.policy. Retry-After is injected by ApiErrors.rateLimitedWithRetry in lib/api/errors.ts, so the route should not set rate-limit headers directly.

Add tests to verify rate limiting happens before handler execution: 60 successful requests followed by the 61st returning 429, with audit_log containing exactly 60 rows because the blocked request never reaches the handler. Also lock schema boundaries with tests for ttl = 7200 accepted, and 7201, 0, -1, and 1.5 rejected.
</info added on 2026-05-21T05:25:40.609Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Vitest for default ttl behavior, ttl > 7200 rejection, and simulated 61st request returning 429 with Retry-After.

subtasks.title: Implement operation-specific presigned URL generation
subtasks.description: Generate the correct presigned URL based on op using decrypted credentials and normalized ttl.
subtasks.details:
Instantiate or reuse the S3/R2 client factory with decrypted credentials, bucket context, and endpoint details from the connection. Route put requests to presignPut(ttl=normalizedTtl), get requests to presignGet(ttl=normalizedTtl), and upload-part requests to presignUploadPart(ttl=normalizedTtl) using uploadId and partNumber. Ensure the response only returns { url, expiresAt } and that the URL is never stored in the database.
<info added on 2026-05-21T05:25:46.599Z>
Use makeS3Client({ accountId, accessKeyId, secretAccessKey }) to create a fresh per-request S3Client in this route; do not cache in lib/r2/client.ts to avoid cross-tenant credential reuse. Dispatch URL generation with switch(input.op) and call presignPut, presignGet, or presignUploadPart directly so the discriminated union preserves narrowing in the upload-part branch without casts for input.uploadId/input.partNumber. Compute ttl once as the normalized value from input.ttl ?? 900 and pass that same ttl into all three helpers instead of handling defaults inside each helper. Return only { url, expiresAt: Date.now() + ttl * 1000 }, with expiresAt as a millisecond epoch so clients do not need timezone assumptions. Wrap the presign phase in a unified catch: write a failure audit entry with op=auditOpFor(op) and status=failure, then map R2CredentialError to 401 via ApiErrors.unauthorized with message "R2 credentials rejected"; rethrow all other errors so withApi handles them as 500. Do not introduce a generic presign(op, client, ...) wrapper, because the helper signatures are intentionally distinct and a wrapper would lose useful type narrowing for upload-part. Keep the switch exhaustive via the discriminated union and no default branch.
</info added on 2026-05-21T05:25:46.599Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Vitest mocking presignPut/presignGet/presignUploadPart to verify each op selects the correct helper and returns url plus expiresAt.

subtasks.title: Write audit logging for presign usage without leaking URLs
subtasks.description: Record a single audit_log entry for each successful or failed presign attempt using the defined op mapping.
subtasks.details:
Call logAudit with userId, connectionId, bucket, key, request metadata, and status. Map op to audit_log.op as presign.get for get and presign.put for both put and upload-part. Do not include the generated presigned URL in audit data or any persistent storage. If audit logging fails, rely on the existing nofail behavior so the API still responds normally.
<info added on 2026-05-21T05:25:53.336Z>
Guarantee exactly one audit_log row for each request that reaches the handler. Requests rejected earlier by withApi interception (429 rate limit, 401 CSRF, 400 Zod validation) are not counted as presign attempts and must not write audit rows.

Add an auditOpFor(input.op) helper that maps get -> presign.get and both put/upload-part -> presign.put, keeping the audit op space narrow to only presign.get and presign.put for presign attempts.

Handle three audit outcomes:
1) Successful presign generation: write status=success with bucket and key.
2) Presign helper throws, including R2CredentialError: write status=failure with bucket/key when available and errorMsg=err.name.
3) Credential decryption fails before a presign attempt: write a single audit row with op=security.decrypt_failed instead of a presign.* op.

Always await logAudit rather than fire-and-forget so the insert flushes before the Worker returns a Response.

Do not pass or persist the signed URL in audit under any circumstance. logAudit should receive no url field, and tests should include a full-table SELECT/JSON stringify assertion that the stored audit data never contains the SIGNED_URL substring.

Do not introduce presign.upload_part into the audit codebook; upload-part is intentionally normalized to presign.put. Do not wrap audit failures into API 500s; rely on lib/audit/log.ts nofail behavior so audit outages do not break the business response. On success audits, omit errorMsg entirely rather than explicitly setting null.
</info added on 2026-05-21T05:25:53.336Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Vitest asserting one audit log call per request with correct op mapping, and confirming upload-part is logged as presign.put.

subtasks.title: Finalize endpoint responses and add comprehensive route tests
subtasks.description: Polish success/error handling and add end-to-end route-level test coverage for the new endpoint.
subtasks.details:
Return JSON responses with proper HTTP status codes for success, validation failures, auth issues, rate limiting, not found, and upstream credential/presign errors. Add route-level Vitest coverage for all three ops producing legal presigned URLs, ttl abuse returning 400, limiter returning 429 plus Retry-After, and audit logging being invoked once. Keep implementation edge-compatible if the surrounding API layer requires it.
<info added on 2026-05-21T05:26:09.046Z>
Split tests into two files: append focused R2PresignSchema unit coverage in tests/unit/api/schemas.test.ts for boundary values, missing required fields, and unknown op; add a new fat integration suite in tests/unit/api/r2-presign-route.test.ts covering full route behavior end to end.

Adopt a fat integration setup with real better-sqlite3 in-memory SQLite, real drizzle-orm/better-sqlite3, and real AES-GCM by seeding credentials through encryptCredential instead of stubbing crypto, so tests catch security regressions such as missing AND userId filtering or incorrect AAD binding. Keep only the outer edges mocked: getSignedUrl, @cloudflare/next-on-pages getRequestContext for env.DB/ENCRYPTION_KEY/AUTH_SECRET injection, next-auth/jwt getToken for session token control, @/lib/auth/adapter createD1Adapter for session existence control, and @/lib/db/client.getDb returning the shared drizzle instance.

Provide a handwritten D1 facade that forwards prepare/bind/first to the same better-sqlite3 connection so rate-limit.ts raw SQL UPSERTs execute against the same database. Use a fixed ENCRYPTION_KEY equal to base64(0x01 repeated 32 times) for deterministic encryption while remaining compatible with keyCache semantics where different raw key strings map to different cache slots.

Integration coverage should include: put/get/upload-part happy paths with url presence and expiresAt asserted within a ±1 second window; ttl=7201 returning 400; upload-part missing partNumber returning 400; nonexistent cid returning 404; cross-user cid access returning 404 without polluting the other user’s audit rows; forced decrypt failure by mutating ciphertext to 0xff repeated 48 returning 500 and writing security.decrypt_failed audit; getSignedUrl throwing InvalidAccessKeyId returning 401 and a presign.put failure audit; 60 successful requests followed by the 61st returning 429 with Retry-After and details.policy = "presign:user:<userId>", while audit rows remain exactly 60; upload-part audit op normalized to presign.put; full-table JSON serialization containing no SIGNED_URL substring to guard against future url persistence; missing session returning 401 auth.unauthorized; and CSRF mismatch returning 401 csrf.invalid.

Prefer real audit row inserts over spy-only audit mocks so op/status/bucket/object_key/userId fields are validated from persisted rows. Prefer real encrypt/decrypt over spying decryptCredential so the invariant that AAD is bound to connection.id is locked by tests.

Implementation pitfalls to account for: when mocking @/lib/db/client, preserve existing schema re-exports via spreading the actual module before overriding getDb, otherwise route imports receive undefined schema; use ulid() to generate nonexistent cids instead of hand-written literals because UlidSchema requires exactly 26 Crockford base32 characters; use "buk" as the shortest valid bucket in tests because BucketNameSchema.min(3) rejects shorter values; and define the discriminated union with shared base shape spread into z.object({...R2PresignBaseShape, op: z.literal(...)}) for clarity and zod 4 alignment.

Validation target: 36 new tests passing, full Vitest suite green, plus clean typecheck, lint, and production build with the route listed at /api/r2/presign.
</info added on 2026-05-21T05:26:09.046Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Route integration-style Vitest with mocked dependencies covering happy paths for put/get/upload-part and all specified failure cases.

tasks.title: Multipart 控制面端点（create / complete / abort）
tasks.description: POST /api/r2/multipart/create、/complete、/abort。这是 Security Invariant #3 的合法服务端调用（只交换元数据，不传字节）。complete 接 parts 列表；abort 用于取消。
tasks.details: 1) create：input { cid, bucket, key, contentType? }，调 createMultipartUpload，返回 { uploadId }；audit op='upload.create'；2) complete：input { cid, bucket, key, uploadId, parts: [{partNumber:number, etag:string}] }，parts.sort by partNumber，调 completeMultipartUpload，返回 { etag, location }；audit op='upload.complete'；3) abort：input { cid, bucket, key, uploadId }，调 abortMultipartUpload，204；audit op='upload.abort'；4) 限流归 'write' 类（600/min/user）；5) CSRF 必须。
tasks.testStrategy: Vitest mock S3：create 返回 uploadId；complete 校验 parts 顺序；abort 调用 SDK 一次；input 校验 parts 至少 1 项；E2E：上传 200MB 文件能成功 create -> upload-part(presigned) -> complete。

subtasks.title: Add multipart request/response Zod schemas in lib/api/schemas.ts
subtasks.description: Define and export the validation schemas needed by the multipart create, complete, and abort control-plane endpoints.
subtasks.details:
Add Zod schemas for create input { cid, bucket, key, contentType? }, complete input { cid, bucket, key, uploadId, parts }, and abort input { cid, bucket, key, uploadId }. Ensure parts is a non-empty array of objects with numeric partNumber and string etag, and add a transform/refinement path suitable for later sorting or route-level sorting. Follow existing schema naming and export conventions already used by presign and list APIs.
<info added on 2026-05-21T14:01:38.796Z>
Implemented in lib/api/schemas.ts:
R2MultipartCreateSchema with { cid, bucket, key, contentType? } and .strict(); contentType now enforces 1..255 chars so empty string is rejected, and strict mode blocks unexpected extra fields.
R2MultipartPartSchema exported as a standalone named schema constant so route code, tests, and future client hooks can share the same multipart part shape.
R2MultipartCompleteSchema with parts length constrained to 1..10_000 to match the S3 spec upper bound. Parts are intentionally not sorted or transformed at schema level; sorting is deferred to lib/r2/control.completeMultipartUpload so the route can forward parts exactly as provided and tests can assert that behavior.
R2MultipartAbortSchema limited to { cid, bucket, key, uploadId } with .strict() to prevent abuse via smuggled fields such as parts.

Rejected alternative: sorting parts via schema .transform() was intentionally not used, because the control wrapper already performs sorting and duplicating that contract in schema would be redundant and would make it harder to express tests asserting that the route does not pre-sort.

Tests added in tests/unit/api/schemas.test.ts: 12 new specs covering create/complete/abort happy paths, strict extra-field rejection, and invalid boundary cases. Verified with pnpm test schemas.test.ts passing. Also avoided the initial ESLint no-unused-vars issue from destructuring out uploadId by switching to an explicit Omit-based/test-data construction approach.
</info added on 2026-05-21T14:01:38.796Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Add schema-focused unit tests or route validation assertions covering valid payloads, missing required fields, and complete.parts minimum length enforcement.

subtasks.title: Implement POST /api/r2/multipart/create route
subtasks.description: Create the multipart upload initialization endpoint using the established secure server-side R2 route pattern.
subtasks.details:
Add app/api/r2/multipart/create/route.ts with edge runtime and wrap the handler with withApi + RateLimitBundles.writeOnlyByUser, enforcing CSRF. Parse input with the new schema, perform user-scoped connection lookup using userId AND cid, decrypt credentials with AES-GCM using AAD=cid, construct makeS3Client per request, call createMultipartUpload with bucket/key/contentType, return { uploadId }, map R2CredentialError to 401, and await audit logging on both success and failure paths with op='upload.create'.
<info added on 2026-05-21T14:01:46.009Z>
Implemented in app/api/r2/multipart/create/route.ts with edge runtime, reusing the same boilerplate pattern as presign/list: user-scoped connection lookup by cid + user_id, parallel AES-GCM credential decrypt with AAD=cid, single per-request makeS3Client construction, createMultipartUpload call, and awaited audit op='upload.create'. Response shape is { uploadId }.

Rate limiting intentionally uses RateLimitBundles.writeOnlyByUser only (the shared 600/min/user write aggregate). There is no separate multipart.create cap because the effective throttle is the subsequent per-part presign limit (60/min/user); one create maps to one upload and is naturally constrained there. This rationale is documented in the route header comment to prevent future confusion about the lack of multipart-specific limiting.

Error handling aligns with presign/list: R2CredentialError maps to 401 and records an awaited 'upload.create' failure audit; decrypt failures map to 500 and record awaited 'security.decrypt_failed' audit. Both paths explicitly await logAudit so the Pages worker does not exit before the audit write completes.

The local asU8(blob) helper is duplicated here as a fourth copy (after presign/buckets/list/create). A TODO was added in the route header: if a fifth route needs the same helper, lift it into lib/db/blob.ts instead of copying again.

Shared connection lookup + decrypt extraction was considered and rejected for now to stay consistent with the existing project style guidance favoring a few similar inline blocks over premature abstraction; revisit only after additional R2 routes make the shared helper clearly justified.
</info added on 2026-05-21T14:01:46.009Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test with mocked S3 client verifying successful uploadId response, 401 mapping for credential failures, CSRF enforcement, and audit invocation on success and error.

subtasks.title: Implement POST /api/r2/multipart/complete route
subtasks.description: Create the multipart completion endpoint that validates and sorts uploaded parts before finalizing the upload.
subtasks.details:
Add app/api/r2/multipart/complete/route.ts following the same edge-runtime route structure and middleware stack. Validate body with the complete schema, resolve the user-owned connection, decrypt credentials with AAD=cid, build the S3 client, sort parts by ascending partNumber before invoking completeMultipartUpload, and return { etag, location }. Apply write-class rate limiting, require CSRF, map R2CredentialError to 401, and await audit logging for both success and failure using op='upload.complete'.
<info added on 2026-05-21T14:01:51.916Z>
Implemented app/api/r2/multipart/complete/route.ts with edge runtime. Response output is normalized to { etag, location } using ?? null because the SDK fields are optional. Added R2MultipartCompleteResponse to lib/api/types.ts, following the existing R2*Response naming, so V2 callers always receive null instead of omitted fields.

Important contract decision: the route does not sort parts before calling lib/r2/control.completeMultipartUpload and forwards parts exactly as provided. Sorting remains inside the control wrapper, which already performs sort([...parts]) by partNumber and documents that the S3 InvalidPartOrder contract belongs there. Keeping sort in the wrapper preserves the “route forwards parts as-given” behavior and avoids duplicating or obscuring responsibility.

Regression coverage was added in r2-multipart-routes.test.ts using out-of-order parts [3,1,2], asserting the mocked control wrapper receives [3,1,2] unchanged from the route. The actual sorting behavior remains covered in tests/unit/r2/control.test.ts.

Error mapping matches the create route: R2CredentialError maps to 401 and logs upload.complete failure; decrypt failure maps to 500 and logs security.decrypt_failed.
</info added on 2026-05-21T14:01:51.916Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test with mocked S3 verifying parts are sent in sorted order, response includes etag/location, invalid empty parts payload is rejected, and audit/error mapping behave as expected.

subtasks.title: Implement POST /api/r2/multipart/abort route
subtasks.description: Create the multipart abort endpoint to cancel an in-progress multipart upload without transferring object bytes.
subtasks.details:
Add app/api/r2/multipart/abort/route.ts using the same secure server-side route conventions as sibling endpoints. Validate request input, fetch the connection with userId AND cid constraints, decrypt access credentials with AAD=cid, create the S3 client, call abortMultipartUpload with bucket/key/uploadId, and return HTTP 204 with no body. Include CSRF enforcement, write-only per-user rate limiting, R2CredentialError to 401 mapping, and awaited audit rows on success/failure with op='upload.abort'.
<info added on 2026-05-21T14:01:59.300Z>
Implemented app/api/r2/multipart/abort/route.ts on edge runtime. The handler returns a bare `new Response(null, { status: 204 })` so the success path stays truly no-body; `withApi` detects `Response` instances, skips default JSON wrapping, and only injects the `x-request-id` header.

Kept rate limiting aligned with the sibling multipart control-plane routes by using `writeOnlyByUser`, so abort/reset cleanup is governed by the same write-class budget as create/complete.

Error handling matches create/complete: `R2CredentialError` maps to 401 and still records awaited failure audit rows with `op='upload.abort'`; decrypt failures map to 500 with `security.decrypt_failed`, also with awaited audit logging.

Deliberately did not add a special 4xx mapping for R2 `NoSuchUpload` on stale/unknown `uploadId`. That case currently flows through `mapR2Error` as `R2UpstreamError` (5xx). This is acceptable because the UI treats abort as fire-and-forget cleanup and does not branch on that condition. If product requirements later need to distinguish “already cleaned up” from genuine failures, extend `lib/r2/errors.ts` with a dedicated mapping then.

Test note: assert `await res.text()` is `""` to verify the 204 response has no body, and assert `res.headers.get("x-request-id")` still matches the expected UUID pattern to confirm trace header injection remains intact for bare `Response` returns.
</info added on 2026-05-21T14:01:59.300Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test with mocked S3 ensuring abortMultipartUpload is called exactly once with expected params, 204 is returned, and CSRF plus credential error handling work properly.

subtasks.title: Add Vitest integration coverage for all multipart control-plane routes
subtasks.description: Create comprehensive integration tests covering create, complete, and abort route behavior and shared security invariants.
subtasks.details:
Add or extend Vitest route tests to cover the three new endpoints using the same mocking style as existing API route tests. Mock S3 SDK calls, connection lookup, decrypt path, and audit writes. Verify create returns uploadId, complete sorts parts before SDK invocation, abort returns 204, write-class rate limit wrapper is wired, CSRF is required, user-scoped connection access is enforced, R2CredentialError maps to 401, and audit is awaited on both success and failure paths.
<info added on 2026-05-21T14:02:10.400Z>
Added tests/unit/api/r2-multipart-routes.test.ts as a single fat integration spec covering create, complete, and abort in one shared fixture setup, matching the existing r2-list-route.test.ts / r2-presign-route.test.ts pattern. The shared setup reuses D1-shaped sqlite, drizzle, AES-GCM encryption fixtures, fake JWT, fake session store, Cloudflare next-on-pages mock, and auth-adapter mock, with ENCRYPTION_KEY_B64 set to 0x07.repeat(32) to keep multipart fixtures grep-friendly and visually distinct from other route suites.

Coverage added:
Create route: happy path returns uploadId, verifies contentType passthrough, allows omitted contentType, and writes awaited upload.create success audit; rejects unknown extra fields via strict schema; rejects non-ULID cid; returns 404 for cross-user connection access to avoid enumeration leaks; returns 401 for unauthenticated and CSRF mismatch; maps decrypt failure to 500 with security.decrypt_failed audit; maps R2CredentialError to 401 with upload.create failure audit.
Complete route: verifies unsorted parts are passed through to the control wrapper unchanged as a regression guard at the route layer; normalizes missing etag/location in the response to null; writes awaited upload.complete success audit; rejects empty parts with 400 at schema layer; rejects missing uploadId with 400; maps R2CredentialError to 401 with upload.complete failure audit.
Abort route: returns 204 with no body while preserving x-request-id header; asserts abortMultipartUpload is called exactly once with expected parameters; writes awaited upload.abort success audit; maps R2CredentialError to 401 with upload.abort failure audit; hosts the shared write-class rate limit coverage because it is the lightest route.

Rate-limit test was optimized to avoid a slow 600-request loop: instead of incrementing through the full writeAggregate budget, the test inserts a preseeded rate_limit_buckets row with count=599 and then sends two requests to assert one success followed by 429, while still checking retry-after, 429 body, and details.policy through the real limiter path.

Test harness fixes and decisions:
Cross-user 404 coverage now stores the returned user object from seedUserAndConnection({ loginAs: true }) and explicitly uses userB.csrfToken, avoiding the earlier fakeJwt.token overwrite bug from repeated loginAs seeding.
Control-layer mocks use top-level vi.fn wrappers for createMultipartUpload, completeMultipartUpload, and abortMultipartUpload and spread them inside vi.mock("@/lib/r2/control"), allowing isolated mockReset calls in beforeEach without cross-test leakage.
Adjusted destructuring patterns to satisfy ESLint no-unused-vars by avoiding underscore-prefixed throwaway bindings and using explicit field selection/Omit style where needed.

tasks.title: Upload Queue store + dispatcher（3×3 并发）
tasks.description: Zustand store 管理上传任务的内存状态机；dispatcher 协程：3 个文件并发，每文件 3 个 part 并发；不持久化（关页面=丢失）；取消即 abort。
tasks.details: 1) stores/upload-queue.ts：task = { id, file, bucket, key, status: 'queued'|'preparing'|'uploading'|'completing'|'done'|'failed'|'canceled', uploadId?, parts: Map<number,{etag?, status}>, progress: {bytesUploaded, totalBytes} }；2) actions: enqueue, cancel, retry, removeDone；3) dispatcher：useEffect 在 layout 启动一个 worker loop，挑 status='queued' 的最早 3 个开跑；4) 单文件 flow：if size < 100MB -> presign put -> fetch PUT；else create multipart -> 切片 10MB part -> 每 part presign upload-part + fetch PUT，3 个并发 -> complete-multipart；5) ETag 从 response header 取（注意 R2 用 ETag header，可能带引号）；6) cancel: AbortController + 调 /api/r2/multipart/abort；7) Progress 用 XMLHttpRequest 而非 fetch（fetch 无上传进度）或用 ReadableStream 上传 + 计算字节数。
tasks.testStrategy: Vitest：单文件 < 100MB 走单 PUT 路径；> 100MB 走 multipart 路径，part 数 = ceil(size/10MB)；并发数测试：5 个文件入队同时只有 3 个进入 uploading；cancel 后 status='canceled' 且调了 abort 端点；E2E：拖 300MB 假文件成功上传到 R2。

subtasks.title: Define the UploadTask state machine and initialize the Zustand store skeleton
subtasks.description: Create the in-memory upload queue store with the exact task status union, task shape, core actions, and adapter helpers required by the upload system.
subtasks.details:
Implement stores/upload-queue.ts with a Zustand store that does not use any persistence middleware. Define the UploadTask internal model with fields id (ulid), file, cid, bucket, key, status, uploadId?, parts as Map<number,{ etag?, status:'pending'|'uploading'|'done'|'failed' }>, bytesUploaded, totalBytes, errorMsg?, abortController?, and createdAt. Add actions enqueue(file,cid,bucket,key), setStatus(id,status), setProgress(id,bytesUploaded), setUploadId(id,uploadId), setPart(id,partNumber,patch), setError(id,msg), cancel(id), retry(id), removeDone(), and removeOne(id). Export a useUploadQueueCount selector and a toUploadDrawerTask(task) adapter that maps internal state for upload-drawer.tsx and computes speed using an EMA sample window.
<info added on 2026-05-21T14:34:15.963Z>
Added enqueueMany(files) to batch-drop multiple files in a single set() call and reduce re-renders. Store implementation uses zustand vanilla create with an in-memory Map<string, UploadTaskInternal> and per-task Map<number, UploadPartState>; all writes rebuild the top-level Map via a shared patchTask() helper so selector updates are observed correctly.

Upload status union is finalized as queued|preparing|uploading|completing|done|failed|canceled. Internal task state also includes _lastSampleTs, _lastSampleBytes, and _emaSpeed for drawer speed estimation, plus a setAbortController(id, abortController) action so the dispatcher can attach cancellation handles after claiming a task.

Progress/speed calculation is centralized in setProgress(): EMA alpha 0.4 with MIN_SAMPLE_INTERVAL_MS = 50 to avoid divide-by-zero/unstable samples from same-frame XHR progress events. toUploadDrawerTask() intentionally maps preparing to queued because the drawer public status union does not expose preparing and this avoids a visible 0% flash before bytes begin flowing.

cancel(), removeOne(), and retry() are implemented idempotently against terminal statuses {done, failed, canceled}. cancel() aborts the stored AbortController from within the Zustand updater, and retry() is permitted for both failed and canceled tasks per UX intent. Dispatcher logic was intentionally kept out of the store file and will live in lib/uploads/dispatcher.ts to preserve store purity and testability. Validation: pnpm typecheck passes.
</info added on 2026-05-21T14:34:15.963Z>

subtasks.title: Implement the browser-only single-file PUT upload runtime helper
subtasks.description: Build the helper for files below the multipart threshold using presign + XMLHttpRequest PUT with progress and abort support.
subtasks.details:
Create lib/uploads/single-put.ts with uploadSinglePut({ task, signal, onProgress }). The helper should call apiFetch to request a presigned PUT URL, then upload the file body via XMLHttpRequest using content-type from file.type. Wire xhr.upload.onprogress to report bytesUploaded, bridge AbortSignal to xhr.abort(), detect aborted vs HTTP vs network vs presign failures through a typed UploadError { kind: 'aborted' | 'presign' | 'network' | 'http' }, and extract the ETag response header while stripping surrounding quotes using etag.replace(/^"|"$/g, ''). Return { etag } on success.
<info added on 2026-05-21T14:46:44.968Z>
Added reusable exports in lib/uploads/single-put.ts for later multipart integration: uploadBodyViaXhr({ url, body, contentType, signal, onProgress }) as the low-level browser XHR PUT primitive returning { etag }, and stripEtagQuotes(raw) as the single shared regex-based ETag normalizer to prevent drift between single-file and per-part uploads.

Implemented UploadError with kind 'aborted' | 'presign' | 'network' | 'http'; because ES2022 Error includes cause and tsconfig enables noImplicitOverride, the class uses public override readonly cause?: unknown.

Abort handling now includes three guards: signal.aborted before presign, another check after presign resolves, and an immediate signal.aborted re-check just before registering the abort listener to cover the microtask race between presign completion and listener attachment. All cancellation paths converge through xhr.onabort and reject as kind='aborted'.

A 200 PUT response without an ETag header is treated as kind='http' with an explicit hint that R2 bucket CORS must expose ETag, matching the expected operator-facing tooltip copy. HTTP failure messages include a truncated xhr.responseText slice limited to 160 chars to preserve useful S3 error codes without bloating the UI.

Progress callbacks are emitted only when ProgressEvent.lengthComputable is true so queue progress never regresses from partial/unknown-length events.

The single PUT entrypoint was kept decoupled from the Zustand store by accepting only { cid, bucket, key, file } instead of the full UploadTaskInternal, avoiding circular import risk and keeping the helper easier to test.

Validation: pnpm typecheck passes.
</info added on 2026-05-21T14:46:44.968Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Mock apiFetch and global XMLHttpRequest to verify progress callbacks, quote-stripped ETag extraction, non-2xx error handling, and signal-driven cancellation.

subtasks.title: Implement the multipart upload runtime helper with 3-part concurrency
subtasks.description: Build the multipart upload flow for large files, including create, per-part uploads, complete, and best-effort abort behavior.
subtasks.details:
Create lib/uploads/multipart.ts with constants PART_SIZE_BYTES = 10 * 1024 * 1024, MULTIPART_THRESHOLD_BYTES = 100 * 1024 * 1024, and PART_CONCURRENCY = 3. Implement uploadMultipart({ task, signal, onPartProgress, onUploadIdReady }) to call POST /api/r2/multipart/create, emit the uploadId via callback, split the file into 10MB slices, and run a worker pool of size 3 using a shared cursor to process parts. For each part, presign op='upload-part', upload via XHR PUT, capture and sanitize ETag headers, and report per-part progress/status. After all parts succeed, POST /api/r2/multipart/complete with parts sorted by partNumber. On abort or any error, attempt POST /api/r2/multipart/abort in a best-effort way without masking the original failure.
<info added on 2026-05-21T14:54:43.678Z>
Implemented lib/uploads/multipart.ts using the shared single-file XHR runtime instead of duplicating PUT/ETag handling: uploadBodyViaXhr is reused for part uploads, and UploadError is reused so dispatcher error branching stays identical across single-put and multipart flows.

Exported constants now include PART_SIZE_BYTES = 10 * 1024 * 1024, MULTIPART_THRESHOLD_BYTES = 100 * 1024 * 1024, PART_CONCURRENCY = 3, and MAX_PART_COUNT = 10_000.

Multipart execution uses a 3-worker pool with a shared synchronous cursor object { next: 1 }; each worker claims the next part via cursor.next++ and exits when all parts are consumed. This avoids prebuilding a task queue while remaining safe under the JS event loop because the increment happens without an await boundary.

Abort handling is linked through an internal AbortController so either the external signal or the first worker failure aborts remaining work. Each worker checks linkedController.signal.aborted before starting its next iteration to short-circuit after cancellation or another worker’s failure. Promise.allSettled is used for worker coordination so all worker failures are collected cleanly and the first non-null rejection reason is surfaced without leaving lagging worker rejections unhandled.

For each UploadPart PUT, Content-Type is explicitly set to application/octet-stream to avoid Safari defaulting unknown blobs to text/plain and causing presigned signature mismatches. onUploadIdReady fires immediately after create succeeds and before any part upload begins so later cancellation can still call the abort endpoint with a valid uploadId.

Best-effort abort is invoked on every failure path, including create-success-then-presign failure, part upload failure, complete failure, and MAX_PART_COUNT overflow. Abort endpoint errors are swallowed so the original upload error remains the surfaced failure; orphaned multipart uploads are expected to be cleaned up by R2 lifecycle rules if abort also fails.

The external abort listener is explicitly removed on all return paths to avoid retaining the caller’s AbortController via closure capture.

Documented multipart presign behavior: part presigns cannot be batch-precomputed into a single reusable signature because each signature is bound to uploadId, partNumber, and expiry. Current expected traffic remains acceptable for the 60/min/user presign limit.

Validation completed with pnpm typecheck passing.
</info added on 2026-05-21T14:54:43.678Z>

subtasks.title: Build the upload dispatcher worker loop with 3-file concurrency
subtasks.description: Create the orchestrator that watches the queue, claims queued tasks, and runs either single PUT or multipart uploads while updating store state.
subtasks.details:
Implement startUploadDispatcher() in stores/upload-queue.ts or lib/uploads/dispatcher.ts with idempotent startup guarded by a module-level boolean. Subscribe to store changes and schedule work whenever there is idle capacity and queued tasks. Enforce FILE_CONCURRENCY = 3 using a Map<taskId, Promise> for in-flight tasks, always selecting the oldest queued task by createdAt and transitioning it to 'preparing' before execution. Create a new AbortController for each claimed task, choose single-put or multipart based on totalBytes, throttle progress updates via requestAnimationFrame or a 200ms debounce, move tasks through 'uploading' and 'completing', and finalize them as 'done', 'failed', or 'canceled' based on typed runtime errors. Keep runtime helpers pure and let the dispatcher own all store mutations.
<info added on 2026-05-21T15:00:47.617Z>
Implemented lib/uploads/dispatcher.ts as the orchestrator layer, keeping stores/upload-queue.ts as a pure data store without runtime/XHR coupling. startUploadDispatcher() is guarded by a module-level started boolean so repeated calls are a no-op, covering React 18 Strict Mode double-invoked effects.

Scheduling is subscribe-driven and coalesced with queueMicrotask() to avoid recursive/deep claim loops when setStatus('preparing') fires nested store updates. Task claiming uses the status transition to 'preparing' as the mutex boundary: findNextQueued() only considers status === 'queued', so already-claimed items are excluded on subsequent ticks. In-flight tracking uses Map<taskId, Promise> and cleanup happens in each promise .finally() so capacity is released before the next scheduling pass, preventing double-claim races.

Added an after-claim safety re-read before runTask(): if a task was canceled after entering 'preparing', the dispatcher bails early instead of spending a presign request. Error handling preserves terminal-state precedence: if the store already shows 'canceled' or 'done', dispatcher error paths do not overwrite it. UploadError with kind === 'aborted' is mapped to status 'canceled' explicitly rather than 'failed'.

Status flow is 'preparing' -> 'uploading' -> 'done' for single-PUT uploads, and 'preparing' -> 'uploading' -> 'completing' -> 'done' for multipart uploads; 'completing' is only used for multipart finalize/complete. Progress updates are throttled to 200ms per task, but the first progress sample bypasses the throttle so UI movement starts immediately. Multipart progress aggregates uploaded bytes via a per-task partBytes Map, and the dispatcher forces a final progress flush to totalBytes so completed uploads always render 100% even if the last transport event undershoots.

Exported _inFlightCountForTest, _inFlightIdsForTest, and _drainForTest to support follow-up concurrency assertions in tests. Validation: pnpm typecheck passes.
</info added on 2026-05-21T15:00:47.617Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Simulate enqueuing 5 tasks and assert only 3 are simultaneously in preparing/uploading states, with correct transitions for success, failure, and cancellation.

subtasks.title: Implement Dropzone file validation and queueing helpers
subtasks.description: Create the core pure helper logic for partitioning files, validating the 5GB limit, and generating upload keys before wiring the UI.
subtasks.details:
Add a helper module used by Dropzone, e.g. validateAndPartitionFiles(files, prefix), that separates accepted vs skipped files based on file.size <= 5*1024^3 and returns queued/skipped counts plus key mapping via `${prefix}${file.name}`. Keep this logic DOM-independent so Vitest can run in node without jsdom.
<info added on 2026-05-21T16:05:31.544Z>
Implement this in lib/uploads/dropzone-utils.ts alongside the other upload helpers, not under components, so the logic stays framework-free and can be tested in Vitest's node environment without jsdom.

Export:
- MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES = 5 * 1024 ** 3
- validateAndPartitionFiles(files: readonly File[]): { accepted: File[]; skipped: { file: File; reason: 'too-large' | 'empty' }[] }
- describeSkipped(...)
- describeQueued(...)
- keyForFile(prefix, file)

Behavior requirements:
- validateAndPartitionFiles must treat the input as readonly and never mutate it.
- Skip files with size === 0 using reason 'empty'.
- Skip files with size > MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES using reason 'too-large'.
- Accept only files with 0 < size <= MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES.
- keyForFile(prefix, file) should return `${prefix}${file.name}` with no URL encoding.

Toast formatting helpers:
- describeSkipped / describeQueued should format the partition results into toast title/description payloads and return null when no toast is needed, so callers can avoid extra branching.
- For a single skipped/queued file, mention the filename directly and wrap it in “”.
- For multiple skipped files of the same/different reasons, use count-based copy instead of listing names to reduce toast noise.
- Use distinct singular vs plural wording for 0/1/N cases.

Rationale to preserve in implementation:
- 5 GiB matches the R2 single-PUT ceiling; no need to raise it further for V1.
- 0-byte files are intentionally rejected because browsers may emit them when users drag directories, and multipart failures are otherwise confusing, even though R2 can store empty objects.
- Do not URL-encode the key here because the presign endpoint signs the raw key and the SDK handles wire encoding later.
</info added on 2026-05-21T16:05:31.544Z>

subtasks.title: Build the Dropzone component with drag-drop and browse support
subtasks.description: Implement the new dropzone UI component that accepts dropped files or manual selection and enqueues valid uploads.
subtasks.details:
Create components/features/upload/dropzone.tsx to wrap a target area, handle dragenter/dragover/dragleave/drop with preventDefault, and expose a hidden multiple file input triggered by a Browse button. Use useUploadQueueStore.enqueueMany(cid, bucket, files, keyForFile), disable interactions when cid or bucket is missing while still rendering guidance text, and show toast.error for skipped >5GB files plus toast.success summarizing queued vs skipped counts after selection or drop.
<info added on 2026-05-21T16:05:40.925Z>
UX requirements:
Use a drag-state overlay with pointer-events: none so child interactions remain intact and the hover/landing feel is correct. Keep a persistent top hint row visible at all times with UploadCloud icon, a visible Browse button, and the text “Max 5 GB · per file”, so Browse stays keyboard-reachable even when the user is not dragging. When dragging files over the target, show the destination bucket/prefix inside the overlay so users understand exactly where files will be uploaded.

Implementation decisions:
Implement drag-and-drop manually with dragenter/dragover/dragleave/drop rather than react-dropzone to avoid extra bundle cost and because validation is custom anyway. Track nested drag enter/leave with a ref counter (dragCounterRef) instead of a boolean, since moving across child boundaries fires dragleave and otherwise causes hover flicker. Implement hasFiles(dataTransfer) to support both DOMStringList in Safari and array-like types in Chrome/Firefox; iterate by index with a for loop rather than relying on includes(). Set dataTransfer.dropEffect = "copy" during dragover so Safari does not show a “not allowed” cursor. The hidden file input should use sr-only + tabIndex={-1} + aria-hidden and be triggered only through the visible Browse button to keep the accessibility tree clean. After handling input.onChange, reset event.target.value = '' so selecting the same file again still triggers change. If cid or bucket is missing, still render the hint row but reject drop/browse interactions and show a toast guiding the user to pick a connection from the header.

Out of scope / not implemented:
Do not add a global page-wide highlight immediately when files enter the page, since it would overlap with the local overlay; consider that in V2. Directory drop blocking is not implemented in V1; Chrome may expose directories via webkitDirectory and that case is intentionally left unsupported for now.
</info added on 2026-05-21T16:05:40.925Z>

subtasks.title: Add upload queue provider effects for auto-removal and unload guard
subtasks.description: Implement provider-side side effects for completed-task cleanup and navigation protection during active uploads.
subtasks.details:
Extend upload-queue-provider.tsx or a dedicated hook to subscribe to store changes, detect newly done tasks, and schedule removeOne/removeDone after 5000ms while clearing timers if a task is retried before expiry. Also attach window.onbeforeunload only when any task is in an in-flight status and remove the handler cleanly on state change or unmount, without affecting canceled tasks.
<info added on 2026-05-21T16:05:53.625Z>
Refactor both side effects into lib/uploads/effects.ts.

Add startAutoRemoveDone() implemented with plain store.subscribe(handleChange) plus a previous Map diff instead of Zustand selector subscribe. Important bug fix: initialize previous as new Map(), not useUploadQueueStore.getState().tasks, otherwise the first handleChange treats prev/current as unchanged and skips scheduling already-existing done tasks; this is the root cause covered by the test "does not re-schedule for tasks already in 'done' on subscribe ticks". Track pending timers in a Map<id, timerHandle>; when a task leaves done (including retry back to queued or dismiss/remove), clear and delete its timer to avoid leaks. Before a timeout removes a task, re-read the latest store state and only then call removeOne; removeOne is idempotent, and this extra guard prevents races.
