Add hooks/use-download.ts using useMutation to POST to /api/r2/presign with op='get', ttl=900, and the required cid/bucket/key inputs. On success, create a temporary <a> element, set href to the returned URL and download to the provided filename, append/click/remove it, and surface failures with a toast. Keep behavior V1-only with no resume support and rely on the browser's built-in large-file download handling.
<info added on 2026-05-21T13:20:02.195Z>
Implemented hooks/use-download.ts as a mutation-based hook: useDownloadObject returns useMutation rather than useQuery so each user click requests a fresh presigned GET URL and no bearer-token URL is cached. The hook is split into exported helpers for testability and parity with useObjects: requestPresignedDownloadUrl wraps apiFetch and returns R2PresignResponse, triggerNativeDownload handles the DOM side effect, and deriveDownloadFilename derives the final path segment from key after trimming trailing slashes, with DownloadObjectInput.filename able to override it.

Added shared typing/TTL plumbing: R2PresignResponse { url; expiresAt } was added to lib/api/types.ts for reuse by future preview/upload flows, and DOWNLOAD_PRESIGN_TTL_SECONDS re-exports R2_PRESIGN_DEFAULT_TTL_SECONDS from lib/api/schemas.ts so the GET presign body always includes ttl: 900.

Native download trigger behavior is pinned to create an <a> with href, download, rel="noopener", and display:none, then appendChild → click → remove. rel="noopener" is included because the presigned target is a cross-origin Cloudflare R2 URL. triggerNativeDownload is also SSR-safe and no-ops when document is unavailable.

Error handling follows project convention: the hook does not show toast itself and instead exposes failures via mutation.error for the calling component/UI layer to render.

Explicit non-goals preserved in implementation: no retry on presign failure, no chunking/resume support, and no ResponseContentDisposition plumbing. Cross-origin handling still relies on bucket-level CORS/browser native behavior. Tests pin the exact presign payload shape { op: "get", cid, bucket, key, ttl: 900 } and the DOM lifecycle order so future refactors do not silently change wire behavior.

subtasks.title: Implement HMAC delete confirm token helper and Vitest coverage
subtasks.description: Create a reusable helper for generating and verifying delete confirmation tokens with 5 minute expiry.
subtasks.details:
Add lib/api/delete-token.ts with functions to compute keysHash via sha256(sort(keys).join('\n')), generate base64url(HMAC-SHA256(secret, `${userId}|${bucket}|${keysHash}|${exp}`)) + '.' + exp, and verify token integrity, expiry, bucket, user, and keys hash using constant-time comparison where possible.
<info added on 2026-05-22T01:29:20.292Z>
Implementation finalized in lib/api/delete-token.ts using AUTH_SECRET as the HMAC secret source instead of introducing a separate SERVER_SECRET, keeping parity with existing next-auth secret handling and avoiding extra ops surface area.

Token format is strictly <base64url(hmac)>.<unix-seconds-exp>, relying on the fact that base64url never contains "." so split logic is unambiguous. The signed payload is `${userId}|${bucket}|${keysHash}|${exp}`.

keysHash is computed as sha256(sort(keys).join("\n")), with an internal keys.slice().sort() to avoid mutating caller arrays. This preserves order-insensitivity while remaining membership- and character-sensitive. "\n" is safe as an R2 key join delimiter.

HMAC key import is cached via a module-level Map, following the same pattern as aes-gcm.ts, to reduce repeated crypto.subtle.importKey overhead.

Verification rejects any invalid token via a single DeleteTokenError type for all failure branches, including malformed format, expiry, tampering, bucket/user/key mismatch, and wrong secret, so no failure reason is leaked to attackers. Constant-time comparison uses timingSafeEqual from lib/auth/csrf.ts where applicable.

Validation guards added: token length must be 16-512 chars, exp must be 1-12 decimal digits, and exp <= now is rejected immediately.

Vitest coverage completed with 23 passing cases including stable sort behavior, membership sensitivity, single-character sensitivity, round-trip success, expiry, bucket/user/keys/signature/exp tampering, 6 malformed token variants, and wrong-secret handling. Test expectations were aligned to the unified error contract by asserting DeleteTokenError instead of reason-specific messages.
</info added on 2026-05-22T01:29:20.292Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Vitest unit tests for valid token, expired token, tampered token, wrong bucket/user mismatch, and stable hash regardless of keys order.

Add schemas.ts constants and request schema: define DELETE_KEYS_MAX = 1000 to match S3 DeleteObjects limit, reuse ObjectKeySchema (length 1..1024, no leading "/"), and make R2DeletePrepareSchema a strict object so unknown fields are rejected.

Route behavior decisions:
- POST /api/r2/delete/prepare only validates/authenticates and issues confirmToken + expiresAt; it must not call R2, must not decrypt credentials, and must not write audit_log because prepare can be cancelled and only confirm should be accounted/audited.
- Still perform user-scoped connection existence check using cid + user_id to avoid cid enumeration across users; query only SELECT id since no credential blob is needed. Treat cross-user/non-owned cid as not found to avoid observable 403 vs 404 differences.
- Apply RateLimitBundles.writeOnlyByUser(600/min), since prepare is the write-path entrypoint and should share the write aggregate bucket to reduce enumeration/abuse.
- Keep existing auth/CSRF/API error conventions; unauthenticated and CSRF mismatch should return 401.

Add test coverage for 8 cases: happy path, does not call R2, does not write audit, cross-user cid returns 404, empty keys rejected, extra fields rejected by strict schema, unauthenticated returns 401, and CSRF mismatch returns 401.
</info added on 2026-05-22T01:37:04.307Z>

subtasks.title: Implement POST /api/r2/delete route with token verification, deleteObjects, and audit logging
subtasks.description: Add the confirm delete API that verifies the token, deletes objects in batch, and writes audit records.
subtasks.details:
Create app/api/r2/delete/route.ts with schema for { cid, bucket, keys[], confirmToken }. Recompute keys hash from sorted keys, parse and verify token expiry and HMAC, then call deleteObjects for the provided keys without recursion. Return { deleted, errors } and write audit entries with op='object.delete' either per key or as one summarized record depending on existing audit utility capabilities.
<info added on 2026-05-22T01:37:53.399Z>
Implementation decisions for confirm route:
Validate confirmToken before any DB lookup or credential decrypt. This is the first gate so forged/replayed tokens are rejected cheaply and do not consume D1 quota. A token issued for a now-deleted connection should still be rejected at token-validation stage as an invalid intent.
Map every DeleteTokenError case to the same response: confirmationRequired with HTTP 412. Do not leak whether the failure was expiry, tampering, or field mismatch.
Keep V1 non-recursive only. UI sends a flat keys array, and ObjectKeySchema already rejects empty keys and keys starting with "/".
Always write exactly one audit row per request with op="object.delete", never one row per key. Put batch counts in errorMsg, for example "3 key(s) deleted" or "1 deleted, 1 failed".
Partial delete is still HTTP 200 and returns the provider-style partial result { deleted, errors }. If errors is non-empty, mark the single audit row status="failure" so partial batches are easy to find in audit queries.
User isolation remains a second gate after token verification: query connection by both cid and user_id. Even with a valid token, cross-user access must fail if the connection row does not belong to the authenticated user.

Test coverage to add:
happy path; accepts forward/original key order; sort-stable hash across different key orders; audit writes exactly one row with count summary; tampered keys -> 412; tampered bucket -> 412; tampered signature -> 412; tampered exp -> 412; cross-user token reuse -> 412; token failure does not hit DB lookup (for example invalid/empty cid still returns 412); decrypt failure -> 500 plus audit; R2CredentialError -> 401 plus audit; partial failure returns 200 and audit status=failure with counts; cross-user cid lookup returns 404.

Cross-user cid 404 test setup note:
Do not try userA cid + userA token + userB session, because prepare itself requires userA auth. Instead, let userB prepare a valid token using userB’s own cid, then during confirm replace cid with userA’s cid. The token still validates because it is bound to userB/bucket/keys/exp, but the connection lookup WHERE (cid=userA AND user_id=userB) returns no row, proving the second defense layer and producing 404.
</info added on 2026-05-22T01:37:53.399Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Vitest route tests for success path, expired token => 400, tampered token => 400, keys order independence, deleteObjects partial failure handling, and audit invocation assertions.

Create useDeleteObjects hook to call prepare first, then confirm with returned token, handle loading/error/toast states, and expose callbacks for success and dialog closing.
<info added on 2026-05-22T01:54:56.964Z>
useDeleteObjects should own the full serial flow of prepare → confirm and must not expose confirmToken to callers, since the UI already has the full delete input and keeping the token inside the mutation simplifies component usage and avoids leaking it into component state.

Use exact bucket-name typing as the human confirmation ritual instead of requiring keys or a literal "DELETE". Bucket name is the user-recognizable noun and aligns with common GH/AWS/CF conventions, while per-key confirmation is too punishing for large multi-select batches.

DeleteDialog should display the first 20 selected keys and then append an overflow line like "…及 N 个其他". The 20-item cap is intentional: enough to catch mistaken multi-selects without making the dialog overly long.

Do not apply optimistic cache patching after delete. Because R2 can return partial failures, optimistic removal would mislead users; instead, rely on query invalidation so the server remains the source of truth.

Add a dedicated partial-failure toast.warning path that summarizes up to the first 3 failed keys. In error mapping, describeError should switch on ApiErrorCode and map confirmation.required to "Confirmation expired".

Implemented POST /api/share/create at app/api/share/create/route.ts with edge-safe flow and failure auditing. Added ShareCreateSchema to lib/api/schemas.ts using z.union([z.literal(3600), z.literal(86400), z.literal(604800)]) instead of refine so clients receive standard union validation errors; also exported SHARE_TTL_SECONDS as const and ShareTtlSeconds type for UI/hooks reuse. Added sha256Hex() based on crypto.subtle.digest("SHA-256") so only url_hash is persisted in shares (64-char hex), never the raw presigned URL.

Route behavior/order is: validate input strictly, require session + CSRF, apply RateLimitBundles.shareCreateByUser so the specific 30/min share-create limit is evaluated before broader write limits, then perform connection lookup scoped to the authenticated user and return 404 for cross-user cid to avoid enumeration. Before presigning, decrypt connection credentials; on decrypt failure return 500 and write security.decrypt_failed audit. Call presignGet with ttlSeconds, and if presign fails still write a share.create failure audit row. After presign success, insert the share row with url_hash and expiresAt; if insert fails, also write a distinct share.create failure audit row. Success response returns { id, url, expiresAt }. Audit op remains existing "share.create"; no AuditOp changes required.

Verified by unit coverage in tests/unit/api/share-routes.test.ts (22 passing cases), including happy path with persisted url_hash hex not equal to url and ttlSeconds forwarded to presignGet; validation failures for ttl=1800, strict rejection of extra fields, leading-slash key rejection, unauthenticated 401, missing CSRF 401; and security cases for cross-user cid -> 404, R2CredentialError -> 401 with audit failure and no insert, and decrypt failure -> 500 with security.decrypt_failed audit.

Confirmed 7d share TTL is valid here: although R2_PRESIGN_MAX_TTL_SECONDS = 7200 applies to the separate R2 presign schema/route, lib/r2/presign.ts passes expiresIn directly to AWS SigV4 and supports up to 604800, so ShareCreateSchema allowing 604800 is correct. No ENCRYPTION_KEY rotation migration needed for shares because only url_hash is stored.
</info added on 2026-05-22T03:35:23.502Z>

subtasks.title: Implement DELETE /api/share/:id to remove share records and write audit logs
subtasks.description: Create the delete endpoint that removes only the database record for a share owned by the current user and records the deletion event.
subtasks.details:
Add an edge route handler for DELETE /api/share/:id. Validate the route param, confirm the record belongs to the authenticated user, delete only the shares table row, and return a success response suitable for the UI. Do not attempt to revoke the already issued presigned URL at the protocol layer. Write an audit log entry with op='share.delete'. Ensure not-found and unauthorized ownership cases are handled safely and consistently.
<info added on 2026-05-22T03:39:36.627Z>
In app/api/share/[id]/route.ts, implement DELETE using the same pathIdFrom() pattern as connections/[id]: because withApi narrows the handler to a single Request, derive the id from req.url instead of relying on Next 15 params context. Validate the extracted path id with a Zod ShareIdParamSchema that enforces ULID format.

Use a single atomic DELETE ... RETURNING query against shares with both id and user_id in the WHERE clause, and return { id, connectionId, bucket, objectKey } from RETURNING for audit logging. On D1 via Drizzle, returning().all() should yield one row for a matched PK delete; zero rows should be treated as not found. This replaces a SELECT-then-DELETE flow and avoids an extra round trip.

Do not distinguish cross-user ownership from true absence: any non-matching id/user combination must return 404, and write a failure audit entry with error_msg="not_found" for enumeration defense rather than exposing whether the share exists under another user. Invalid non-ULID ids should return 400. Unauthenticated requests should return 401.

On success, delete only the shares table row, return a UI-friendly success response, and write an audit log with op='share.delete' including bucket, objectKey, and connectionId from the deleted row. Preserve the explicit security note in code comments/API contract/UI copy that DELETE cannot revoke an already issued presigned URL at the protocol layer; it only removes the bookkeeping record.

Test coverage to add:
successful delete removes the row and writes a success audit containing bucket, key, and connectionId;
cross-user delete attempt by userB against userA share returns 404, leaves userA row intact, and writes a failure audit for userB with not_found;
invalid non-ULID id returns 400;
unauthenticated delete returns 401.
</info added on 2026-05-22T03:39:36.627Z>

subtasks.title: Create preview kind detection module and shared preview constants
subtasks.description: Implement a pure utility module to classify object keys into image, text, or unavailable preview kinds based on file extension.
subtasks.details:
Add lib/files/preview.ts with normalized lowercase extension parsing, exported image/text extension sets, detectPreviewKind(key): 'image' | 'text' | 'unavailable', and shared constants for text preview cap = 1048576 bytes and large image skeleton threshold = 10485760 bytes.
<info added on 2026-05-22T12:16:44.590Z>
Implemented lib/files/preview.ts with extension-only preview detection and centralized shared constants. Detection intentionally does not use MIME sniffing or response content-type because R2 presigned GET does not reliably reflect a trusted server-set type, and upload metadata is user-controlled; extension matching is sufficient for V1. Dotfiles are excluded from text preview by returning no extension when the only dot is the leading character, preventing inline preview of files like .env; this behavior is pinned in tests. Exported constants now include PREVIEW_TEXT_BYTE_CAP = 1048576, PREVIEW_IMAGE_LARGE_BYTES = 10485760, and PREVIEW_PRESIGN_TTL_SECONDS = 300 so hooks and dialog code import the same values without drift. SVG remains classified as image; although it can execute DOM content, the presigned URL is served from the separate r2.cloudflarestorage.com origin and cannot access app cookies. Extension parsing avoids false matches from dots in folder names by slicing after the last "/" before checking the final "."; this prevents keys like v1.0/file from being misread as having extension "0/file".
</info added on 2026-05-22T12:16:44.590Z>

subtasks.title: Build preview hook helpers for presigning and ranged text fetching
subtasks.description: Create the hook layer that requests preview presigned URLs and fetches the first 1MB of text content using HTTP Range headers.
subtasks.details:
Add hooks/use-preview.ts with a useMutation-style presignPreviewUrl helper calling /api/r2/presign using op='get' and ttl=300, plus a fetchTextHead(url) helper that sends Range: bytes=0-1048575, decodes UTF-8 text, and returns text plus truncation metadata derived from Content-Range or known object size. Export pure request-building/fetch helpers to simplify unit testing.
<info added on 2026-05-22T12:16:53.214Z>
Implemented hooks/use-preview.ts following the use-download.ts pattern. Exported pure helpers requestPreviewPresignedUrl, fetchTextHead, and parseContentRangeTotal so vitest can assert request/response wire behavior without mounting React; the hook uses useMutation so preview dialogs can observe isPending and error consistently with ShareDialog. Presign requests pin ttl=300 seconds and tests cover that value.

fetchTextHead issues Range: bytes=0-1048575 for an exact 1 MiB cap. It defensively slices the received ArrayBuffer to the cap before decoding so oversized bodies are never stored in React state even if an intermediary ignores Range and returns the full object. UTF-8 decoding uses TextDecoder with fatal:false so a multibyte character split at the 1 MiB boundary produces U+FFFD instead of throwing.

Truncation/size metadata behavior:
- totalBytes is derived from Content-Range on 206 responses, or Content-Length on 200 responses.
- The UI may still fall back to a caller-provided sizeHint when headers do not provide total size.
- truncated=true when totalBytes exceeds the cap, or when the raw payload fills the cap and total size is unknown, to stay conservative.

Also handled the 206 Partial Content success path explicitly rather than treating it as an error.
</info added on 2026-05-22T12:16:53.214Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Vitest tests for presign request payload shape, ttl value, Range header presence, and text result parsing with mocked fetch responses.

 for text files, show a banner when only the first 1MB is shown, render an unavailable message for unsupported types, and show a loading skeleton for large images over 10MB. Ensure content is lazy-mounted only when the dialog opens.

Implemented components/features/files/preview-dialog.tsx with lazy mounting gated by open using `{props.open ?  : null}`, matching ShareDialog behavior and relying on DialogContent unmount on close so presigned URL state is naturally discarded without a reset effect. Split rendering into ImageView, TextView, UnavailableView, plus PresignLoadingView while the presign request is in flight. Kept the large-image loading skeleton only for files larger than 10 MiB, clearing it on img onLoad/onError to avoid skeleton flash for small images. TextView is keyed by URL so its initial loading state re-seeds on presigned URL changes without triggering the react-hooks/set-state-in-effect lint violation; the ranged text fetch also uses AbortController for cleanup on unmount. The truncation banner is shown when `truncated || (sizeHint > cap)` so object size remains a fallback signal if the proxy strips both Content-Range and Content-Length. Added an inline Download action next to Close using the existing useDownloadObject hook so unsupported previews can still be downloaded directly. Pitfall noted: ESLint blocks setting loading state at the top of useEffect, so re-mounting by URL key was used instead of resetting state inside the effect.

subtasks.title: Decide and document edge-runtime test runner posture for Vitest
subtasks.description: Evaluate whether adding `@cloudflare/vitest-pool-workers` is necessary now, and document that the current Node environment remains sufficient unless a real edge-runtime gap is identified.
subtasks.details:
Inspect the current `vitest.config.ts`, existing test coverage of route handlers, and any edge-specific APIs in use. Record a concise decision that `environment='node'` is acceptable for the current suite, while noting `@cloudflare/vitest-pool-workers` as a future enhancement rather than an immediate requirement.
<info added on 2026-05-22T11:45:40.900Z>
Decision documented: keep vitest `environment='node'` for the current suite and do not add `@cloudflare/vitest-pool-workers` in this task. Existing route-handler tests already import `app/api/**/route.ts` directly and invoke handlers with constructed `Request`/`NextRequest` objects, so they do not rely on workerd-specific runtime behavior. D1 coverage is already represented through `better-sqlite3` plus Drizzle migrations in `tests/unit/db/migration.test.ts` and `tests/unit/api/*.test.ts`, exercising the same SQL surface used by D1. Web Crypto coverage is also sufficient under Node 22, where `crypto.subtle.encrypt/decrypt` and `getRandomValues` are available; the AES-GCM tests already cover AAD, IV, and tag-related paths without requiring a worker pool. Adding `@cloudflare/vitest-pool-workers` now would introduce roughly 50MB of additional dependencies, slow CI installs, and mostly duplicate issues that would already surface in `pnpm preview` or on Pages. Revisit this as a future enhancement only if the test suite gains real workerd-only scenarios such as Durable Objects, R2 bindings, or KV. Action: leave `vitest.config.ts` unchanged; no code change required.
</info added on 2026-05-22T11:45:40.900Z>

subtasks.title: Install Playwright and create the base E2E configuration
subtasks.description: Add Playwright dependencies, scripts, and a working configuration for local and CI end-to-end runs.
subtasks.details:
Install @playwright/test and required browsers, add a test:e2e script, and create playwright.config.ts with webServer running `pnpm preview`, baseURL set to `http://localhost:8788`, storageState support, retries/timeouts, and separate setup project wiring for auth reuse.
<info added on 2026-05-22T11:10:06.811Z>
Completed setup with @playwright/test@1.60.0. Browser download is intentionally deferred by installing with PLAYWRIGHT_SKIP_BROWSER_DOWNLOAD=1 so regular pnpm install stays faster/smaller in CI; browsers are installed later via a dedicated one-time step.

playwright.config.ts decisions:
- baseURL defaults to http://localhost:8788 and can be overridden by E2E_BASE_URL so CI can point at a different wrangler dev port or staging.
- webServer runs `pnpm preview` instead of `pnpm dev` because local Next dev lacks D1 bindings and causes /api/* 500s; wrangler/pages preview better mirrors production per CLAUDE.md local-dev guidance.
- Define three projects: setup (runs auth.setup.ts), chromium (depends on setup and uses storageState), and chromium-unauth (runs login.spec.ts without storageState) so login coverage always starts from a truly unauthenticated state.
- Set fullyParallel=false and workers=1 in CI because E2E writes to a real shared R2 bucket and must avoid cross-test file collisions.
- Default trace=retain-on-failure and screenshot/video=only-on-failure to balance debuggability vs artifact size.
- Support E2E_NO_WEBSERVER as an escape hatch to skip Playwright-managed webServer when preview is already started manually for faster local iteration.

package.json now includes:
- test:e2e
- test:e2e:install -> playwright install --with-deps chromium

Repo hygiene:
- Add /playwright/.auth to .gitignore.
- Ignore playwright/.auth/** in ESLint.

Runbook note: pnpm onlyBuiltDependencies does not run @playwright/test postinstall by default, so browsers are not auto-downloaded. This is desirable for cache-friendly CI, but contributors and CI must run `pnpm test:e2e:install` before executing specs.
</info added on 2026-05-22T11:10:06.811Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `pnpm exec playwright test --list` and `pnpm preview` to verify the config boots successfully and the webServer becomes reachable at localhost:8788.

subtasks.title: Implement reusable authenticated storageState setup fixture
subtasks.description: Create a Playwright auth setup flow that logs in once and persists admin session state for later specs.
subtasks.details:
Add `tests/e2e/auth.setup.ts` to perform a real login using seeded admin credentials, wait for post-login navigation to stabilize, and write storage state to `playwright/.auth/admin.json`. Update config projects or use dependencies so other specs load this storageState automatically.
<info added on 2026-05-22T11:11:20.048Z>
Implemented reusable auth setup in tests/e2e/auth.setup.ts and wired storageState reuse for downstream specs. Admin credentials are read strictly from E2E_ADMIN_EMAIL and E2E_ADMIN_PASSWORD and must match the pair inserted by seed-admin.ts into D1; missing env now throws immediately instead of falling back to placeholder defaults. The login flow uses getByRole/getByLabel selectors rather than brittle CSS selectors. Because LoginForm is wrapped in Suspense for useSearchParams SSR requirements, the setup first waits for the "Sign in" heading before filling fields to avoid interacting with the skeleton state. Successful login assertion is based on expect(page).not.toHaveURL(/\/login(\?|$)/), which is more robust than waiting for a fixed destination because router.replace may land on / or /buckets. The persisted state written to playwright/.auth/admin.json captures both the Auth.js v5 httpOnly session cookie and the readable CSRF cookie created by GET /api/csrf, so later specs calling mutating endpoints through lib/api/client.ts can construct X-CSRF-Token correctly. The auth file path remains ignored by git and must never be committed.
</info added on 2026-05-22T11:11:20.048Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Execute only the auth setup project and confirm `playwright/.auth/admin.json` is generated and can be reused without re-entering credentials.

subtasks.title: Add login E2E coverage for failed and successful authentication
subtasks.description: Create login tests that validate both rejection behavior and successful redirect behavior.
subtasks.details:
Implement `tests/e2e/login.spec.ts` to cover invalid credentials showing an error message and valid credentials redirecting into the dashboard. Ensure the spec can run independently from the storageState flow when needed and uses stable selectors and assertions for navigation and flash/toast states.
<info added on 2026-05-22T11:13:43.696Z>
Completed `tests/e2e/login.spec.ts` with two cases: invalid password does not leak account existence and successful login exits `/login`. Added `beforeAll` validation for `E2E_ADMIN_EMAIL` and `E2E_ADMIN_PASSWORD` that throws on missing values instead of skipping, so CI cannot false-pass. The invalid-login case uses a sufficiently long fake password to bypass the client-side disabled submit guard in `login/page.tsx` and ensure the request reaches the server. The success case avoids binding to a specific post-login destination and only asserts `not.toHaveURL(/\/login/)`, matching `router.replace(callbackUrl ?? "/")` behavior. Error assertions target the `ErrorBanner` via `role=alert` and the literal heading `Sign-in failed`, explicitly avoiding direct checks on backend error code strings to prevent silent i18n drift.
</info added on 2026-05-22T11:13:43.696Z>

subtasks.title: Cover connection creation flow and masked credential listing
subtasks.description: Write E2E tests for adding an R2 connection and verifying the resulting list view behavior.
subtasks.details:
Implement `tests/e2e/connections.spec.ts` to open the settings page, submit the add-connection form with valid test credentials, optionally exercise connection validation behavior, and assert the new row appears with the expected name/account info and masked access key rather than raw secret material.
<info added on 2026-05-22T11:13:49.314Z>
Skip the entire describe when any of E2E_R2_ACCOUNT_ID, E2E_R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID, or E2E_R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY is missing, because POST /api/connections performs a real ListBuckets probe and placeholder credentials are not useful.

Use a unique connection name like `e2e-${Date.now().toString(36)}` so repeated local/CI runs do not collide.

Strengthen the assertion as an end-to-end guard for Security Invariant #2: the rendered row text must include `****`, but must never contain the raw ACCESS_KEY or SECRET_KEY values anywhere in the browser-visible content.

Wait up to 30s for the success toast `Connection added` to account for wrangler cold start and the upstream R2 credential probe.

Add a `deleteConnectionRow` helper for self-cleanup after the test. The row action button uses a dynamic aria-label `Delete <name>`, the confirmation input label is dynamic (`Type <name> to confirm`) so match it with `/to confirm/i`, and the final destructive button text is `Delete connection` rather than just `Delete`.

subtasks.title: Implement browse, small upload, large upload, and download E2E flow
subtasks.description: Create end-to-end coverage for bucket browsing plus upload and download operations, including multipart-sized files.
subtasks.details:
Add `tests/e2e/browse-upload-download.spec.ts` to select the configured connection, browse buckets, upload a small file, upload a large file that exercises multipart behavior if supported by the app path, and download an object with content verification. Use deterministic fixture files and cleanup naming to avoid collisions in CI.
<info added on 2026-05-22T11:24:06.239Z>
Implemented tests/e2e/browse-upload-download.spec.ts plus shared tests/e2e/fixtures.ts helpers.

fixtures.ts extracts readE2EEnv, ensureTestConnection, setActiveConnection, uploadObjectViaApi, and browseHref. It reads E2E_R2_ACCOUNT_ID, E2E_R2_ACCESS_KEY, E2E_R2_SECRET_KEY, and E2E_R2_BUCKET. ensureTestConnection creates the connection via POST /api/connections instead of reusing the UI flow, since that path is already covered in connections.spec.ts and the API path is more stable here.

Shared helper details:
CSRF cookie name must be csrf, matching lib/auth/csrf-constants.ts, not csrf-token.
Zustand persist storage payload must be shaped as { state, version: 2 } so stores/active-connection.ts migration does not reset activeBucket to null.
Presign requests use ttl, not ttlSeconds, and do not accept contentType because that belongs on the PUT request header.
Object deletion uses the prepare -> delete two-step flow, with confirmToken as the field name and /api/r2/delete as the final endpoint, not /confirm.

Spec coverage:
Upload uses page.waitForEvent("filechooser") with chooser.setFiles through the Dropzone browse button to exercise the real UI path and trigger CORS preflight.
Download uses page.waitForEvent("download"), reads download.path(), and compares Buffer content to verify presigned GET round-trip integrity.
Upload and download cases generate unique keys via stamp() and clean up with deleteObjectViaApi after each run so the throwaway bucket does not accumulate objects.

Important boundary:
Resolve baseURL from testInfo.project.use.baseURL rather than page.context()._options.baseURL, since the latter is a private API.
</info added on 2026-05-22T11:24:06.239Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Run the spec and assert objects appear after upload, downloaded bytes match expected content, and the large file path completes successfully under preview.

subtasks.title: Add delete flow coverage with typed confirmation and bulk delete behavior
subtasks.description: Test destructive object deletion safeguards and successful deletion outcomes for single and multiple selections.
subtasks.details:
Implement `tests/e2e/delete.spec.ts` to verify delete actions remain disabled or blocked until the exact typed confirmation is entered, then confirm object removal from the listing. Include a batch deletion scenario for multiple objects to ensure selection state and confirmation UX work correctly.
<info added on 2026-05-22T11:24:13.293Z>
Implemented tests/e2e/delete.spec.ts with three cases covering typed confirmation plus single and bulk delete flows. Case 1 verifies accidental-delete protection: typing a bucket name with one character wrong keeps the Delete button disabled, then closes via Cancel and performs API cleanup so the bucket is not polluted. Case 2 verifies single delete success: typing the exact bucket name enables the dialog action, clicking it shows the "1 object deleted" toast and removes the row from the listing; no API cleanup is needed because the object should be gone. Case 3 verifies bulk delete: uploads 3 objects under the same prefix, uses the header "Select all rows on this page" checkbox, asserts the SelectionBanner shows "3 selected", clicks the SelectionBanner Delete action, types the bucket name, clicks the exact "Delete 3 objects" dialog button, waits for the "3 objects deleted" toast, and confirms all three rows disappear.

Added locator notes for a UI pitfall: the SelectionBanner Delete button in object-table is a different element from the row hover Delete action. In bulk delete, selecting by aria-label "Delete" on a row does not work because the SelectionBanner uses button text while the row action uses aria-label, so use page.getByRole("button", { name: /^delete$/i }).first() to target the banner button. Also matched DeleteDialog action labels precisely because they vary by selection count: "Delete object" for a single object and "Delete N objects" for bulk delete, using regex for exact assertions.
</info added on 2026-05-22T11:24:13.293Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Run the delete spec and verify incorrect confirmation text prevents deletion, correct text enables deletion, and removed objects no longer appear after refresh.

subtasks.details:

Use authenticated setup plus pre-created test objects from the browse/upload flow or create an isolated object within the spec for independence.
<info added on 2026-05-22T11:24:18.319Z>
Completed in tests/e2e/share.spec.ts as a single end-to-end case covering mint -> /shares list -> delete record. Start from the row-hover Share button with aria-label "Share", choose the 1h TTL option to minimize quota footprint, then click "Create link". Use the post-mint heading "Share link ready" as the synchronization point for the stage transition. Assert that the URL field with aria-label "Presigned share URL" has a value matching https://<accountid>.r2.cloudflarestorage.com/... and that it contains encodeURIComponent(filename); this serves as a catch-all for silent hijack/path mismatch regressions. In /shares, delete via the row action with aria-label "Delete share record", confirm the dialog heading is "Remove share record?", submit with the button text "Delete record", and verify the success toast "Record deleted". Do not attempt to fetch/download the generated URL, since downloadability is a CDN contract rather than an app concern and exercising a still-valid 1h URL would only waste quota.

subtasks.title: Update CORS, multipart cleanup, deployment, and security invariant documentation
subtasks.description: Deliver the documentation set covering R2 CORS, multipart lifecycle cleanup, deployment runbook, README links, and the CLAUDE.md security footnote.
subtasks.details:
Create `docs/r2-cors.md` with the standard CORS JSON example using `AllowedOrigins=[NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL]`, `AllowedMethods=[GET, PUT]`, `AllowedHeaders=[*]`, and `MaxAgeSeconds=3600`. Add `docs/multipart-cleanup.md` recommending the R2 lifecycle rule to abort incomplete multipart uploads after 7 days. Add `docs/deploy-runbook.md` documenting env setup including `ENCRYPTION_KEY=\`openssl rand -base64 32\``, first admin seed steps, D1 migration application, and `wrangler pages deploy`. Update `CLAUDE.md` Security Invariant #3 with the exact exception footnote for control-plane R2 calls, and update `README.md` with a quick entry to CORS and deployment guidance. Completed. Documentation set, README links, `.dev.vars.example`, and the CLAUDE.md footnote are now in place. CLAUDE.md Security Invariant #3 now includes the exact `
**Exception:**` footnote for control-plane R2 calls; `grep "Exception.*control-plane R2 calls"` matches exactly once. `docs/r2-cors.md` now includes the recommended CORS JSON with `AllowedOrigins=[NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL]`, `AllowedMethods=[GET, PUT]`, `AllowedHeaders=[*]`, `ExposeHeaders=[ETag]`, and `MaxAgeSeconds=3600`, plus Wrangler apply commands, multi-environment examples, and a troubleshooting table. The `ExposeHeaders=ETag` requirement is explicitly emphasized because multipart upload completion requires reading each part ETag before `CompleteMultipartUpload`. `docs/multipart-cleanup.md` now recommends a 7-day `AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload` lifecycle rule, explains the 7-day tradeoff (`<1d` too noisy, `>30d` wastes money), and includes Wrangler commands plus `audit_log` troubleshooting guidance. `docs/deploy-runbook.md` now provides an 11-step runbook covering install, `ENCRYPTION_KEY` generation, local `.dev.vars`, D1 creation, migrations, admin seeding, local smoke test, Pages configuration, production migrate/seed, deployment, R2 bucket CORS+lifecycle setup, and optional E2E verification. It also ends with a dedicated 5-step `ENCRYPTION_KEY` rotation procedure that requires re-encrypting `connections` rows before switching keys. `README.md` now includes a top-level overview, quick start, mandatory per-bucket configuration notes linking to `docs/r2-cors.md` and `docs/multipart-cleanup.md`, and test commands. Added `.dev.vars.example` template and excluded it via `.prettierignore` because Prettier cannot infer a parser for shell-style `KEY=value` files. The file uses `#` comments only; `//` comments are not valid for this template format. Pitfall captured: without adding `.dev.vars.example` to `.prettierignore`, `pnpm format:check` fails with `No parser could be inferred`. Verification completed: `pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build` all pass, 510 unit tests are green, and `playwright test --list` shows 10 E2E cases across the `setup`, `chromium`, and `chromium-unauth` projects.
